Statics & Equilibrium

150 diagrams across 15 lessons

Forces as Vectors 10

u1-L1-Q1 multiple-choice

Why can't you find the resultant of concurrent forces by subtracting the smallest from the largest?

u1-L1-Q2 multiple-choice

Why resolve 3D concurrent forces into x, y, z components instead of working with magnitudes and angles?

u1-L1-Q3 true-false

The resultant of a non-concurrent force system can always be reduced to a single force.

u1-L1-Q4 multiple-choice

Direction cosines cosα=0.6, cosβ=0.7, cosγ=0.5 are given for a 3D force. What should you verify first?

u1-L1-Q5 multiple-choice

How do you find the moment of an angled force when the perpendicular distance is hard to compute?

u1-L1-Q6 fill-blank

A couple produces a pure moment with zero net force. Unlike the moment of a single force, the moment of a couple is the same about _____ point in space.

u1-L1-Q7 multiple-choice

Two forces of 100 N each act at a point. One is directed along the positive x-axis, and the other at 60° from the x-axis. What is the magnitude of their resultant?

u1-L1-Q8 multiple-choice

A 500 N force acts at 30° above the horizontal. What are its horizontal (Fx) and vertical (Fy) components?

u1-L1-Q9 true-false

Two forces of equal magnitude acting in opposite directions along the same line of action always produce a couple.

u1-L1-Q10 multiple-choice

Three concurrent forces in 2D are in equilibrium. If you know two of the forces completely, what can you determine about the third?

Moments and Couples 10

u1-L1-Q11 multiple-choice

A force F has components Fx = -40 N and Fy = 30 N. What is the angle of the force measured counterclockwise from the positive x-axis?

u1-L1-Q12 multiple-choice

When should you use the cross product r × F to compute a moment instead of the scalar formula M = Fd?

u1-L1-Q13 true-false

The moment of a force about a point depends on the choice of coordinate system used to describe the position and force vectors.

u1-L1-Q14 multiple-choice

What is the unit vector from A(1,2,3) to B(4,6,3)?

u1-L1-Q15 multiple-choice

What is the physical significance of the dot product of two force vectors F1 · F2?

u1-L1-Q16 fill-blank

Varignon's theorem states that the moment of a force about a point equals the sum of the _____ of its components about the same point.

u1-L1-Q17 multiple-choice

Three parallel forces: 10 N up at x=0, 20 N down at x=3 m, 10 N up at x=6 m. What is the resultant?

u1-L1-Q18 multiple-choice

How many independent scalar equilibrium equations exist for a 2D rigid body?

u1-L1-Q19 true-false

If three non-parallel, non-concurrent coplanar forces are in equilibrium, their lines of action must all pass through a single common point.

u1-L1-Q20 multiple-choice

What is the magnitude of F = (30i + 40j - 120k) N?

Equilibrium Equations 10

u1-L1-Q21 multiple-choice

What is a "wrench" in force system reduction?

u1-L1-Q22 multiple-choice

Two cables exert forces on an eye bolt. Cable A pulls with 800 N at 30° above horizontal to the right, and Cable B pulls with 600 N at 120° from the positive x-axis. What is the x-component of the resultant force?

u1-L1-Q23 true-false

The resultant of two concurrent forces can never exceed the sum of their magnitudes.

u1-L1-Q24 multiple-choice

A force of 1000 N must be resolved into two components along directions that are 40° and 65° from the force vector (the two directions are 105° apart). Using the sine rule on the force triangle, what is the component along the 40° direction?

u1-L1-Q25 multiple-choice

A 50 N horizontal force acts at the end of a 2 m bar at 30° from horizontal. What is the moment about the pivot?

u1-L1-Q26 fill-blank

The principle of _____ states that the external effect of a force on a rigid body is the same regardless of where the force is applied along its line of action.

u1-L1-Q27 multiple-choice

A moment of 200 N·m clockwise and a moment of 150 N·m counterclockwise act on the same rigid body. What is the net moment?

u1-L1-Q28 multiple-choice

How many independent equilibrium equations exist for a 3D rigid body?

u1-L1-Q29 true-false

Lami's theorem can be applied to a system of four concurrent forces in equilibrium.

u1-L1-Q30 multiple-choice

To move force F from point A to point B (off its line of action), what must be added?

Drawing Free Body Diagrams 10

u1-L2-Q1 multiple-choice

a junior engineer's FBD of a cantilever beam and notice they drew only a vertical and horizontal reaction force at the wall, omitting the reaction moment. What consequence would this error have on their analysis?

u1-L2-Q2 true-false

A roller on an inclined surface provides a reaction perpendicular to the surface, not always vertical.

u1-L2-Q3 multiple-choice

What two things must be correct when replacing a distributed load with a single resultant force?

u1-L2-Q4 multiple-choice

A continuous beam over three supports is statically indeterminate. A designer proposes inserting an internal hinge in one span to make it determinate. Why does this work?

u1-L2-Q5 multiple-choice

Simply-supported beam, span 8 m, 20 kN point load at 3 m from left. What is the left reaction?

u1-L2-Q6 fill-blank

When you take moments about one support of a simply-supported beam, the reaction at that support drops out of the equation because its moment arm is _____.

u1-L2-Q7 multiple-choice

A pin support in 2D provides how many reaction force components?

u1-L2-Q8 multiple-choice

Boom crane: bar pinned at one end, cable at the other, load W hanging. How many unknowns on the FBD?

u1-L2-Q9 true-false

Internal forces between connected parts of a system must be shown on the FBD of the entire system.

u1-L2-Q10 multiple-choice

Where does a uniform beam's self-weight act on its FBD?

Support Reactions 10

u1-L2-Q11 multiple-choice

Beam span 6 m, UDL of 10 kN/m over full length. Reactions at each support?

u1-L2-Q12 multiple-choice

What is a two-force member and why does it matter in FBDs?

u1-L2-Q13 true-false

A smooth surface can exert both normal and tangential forces on a contacting body.

u1-L2-Q14 multiple-choice

Triangular load from 0 to w₀ over span L. What is the resultant magnitude and location?

u1-L2-Q15 multiple-choice

A beam has 4 unknown reactions but only 3 equilibrium equations. What does this mean?

u1-L2-Q16 fill-blank

A member that has forces applied at only two points (and no other loads) is called a _____-force member.

u1-L2-Q17 multiple-choice

On a three-force member in equilibrium, two forces intersect at a point. What about the third?

u1-L2-Q18 multiple-choice

Which 2D support provides a force that can act in any direction?

u1-L2-Q19 true-false

The weight of a body should always be included on its FBD, even if a problem statement says to "neglect the weight of the beam."

u1-L2-Q20 multiple-choice

Span 10 m, loads 15 kN at 2 m and 25 kN at 7 m from left. Left reaction?

Solving Beam Problems 10

u1-L2-Q21 multiple-choice

What internal forces/moments must appear at a cut section in a 2D member?

u1-L2-Q22 multiple-choice

How is rope tension shown on the FBD of a frictionless pulley?

u1-L2-Q23 fill-blank

A structure is called a _____ if it has fewer support reactions than the number of equilibrium equations, making it unable to maintain equilibrium under general loading.

u1-L2-Q24 multiple-choice

Cable at 45° attached to the free end of a cantilever. What reaction does it provide?

u1-L2-Q25 true-false

A fixed support at a wall can be modeled as a combination of a pin support plus a couple (moment reaction).

u1-L2-Q26 multiple-choice

Pin at A, roller at B (horizontal), horizontal load P at midspan. Properly constrained?

u1-L2-Q27 multiple-choice

What is the first step in solving any statics problem?

u1-L2-Q28 multiple-choice

Uniform plank (weight W) on two end supports. Person (weight P) at L/4 from left. Left reaction?

u1-L2-Q29 true-false

If a structure has three non-parallel, non-concurrent reaction forces, it is always properly constrained in 2D.

u1-L2-Q30 fill-blank

A link or strut that is pinned at both ends and carries no load between the pins is a classic example of a two-force member, meaning the internal force acts purely along the member's _____.

Truss Fundamentals 10

u1-L3-Q1 multiple-choice

m=11, j=7, r=3 for a 2D truss. What does m+r vs. 2j tell you?

u1-L3-Q2 true-false

In the method of sections, you may cut through at most three members with unknown forces to solve a 2D truss section directly.

u1-L3-Q3 multiple-choice

Why shouldn't you remove a zero-force truss member to save material?

u1-L3-Q4 multiple-choice

Truss vs. rigid frame for a roof; what is the key structural difference?

u1-L3-Q5 multiple-choice

How do you efficiently find one member force deep inside a large truss?

u1-L3-Q6 fill-blank

In truss analysis, the method of _____ finds all member forces sequentially, while the method of _____ targets specific members by cutting through the truss.

u1-L3-Q7 multiple-choice

At an unloaded truss joint with only two non-collinear members, what are their forces?

u1-L3-Q8 multiple-choice

Unloaded joint, three members, two collinear. Force in the third member?

u1-L3-Q9 true-false

The method of joints can only be started at a joint with two or fewer unknown member forces.

u1-L3-Q10 multiple-choice

In a Pratt truss under gravity loading, what state are the diagonals in?

Method of Joints 10

u1-L3-Q11 multiple-choice

Why take moments about the intersection of two cut members in the method of sections?

u1-L3-Q12 multiple-choice

m=9, j=6, r=4 for a truss. Classification?

u1-L3-Q13 true-false

In a simple truss, every member is assumed to be connected at its ends by frictionless pins, so members carry only axial forces (no bending or shear).

u1-L3-Q14 multiple-choice

What distinguishes a frame from a truss in structural analysis?

u1-L3-Q15 multiple-choice

Why must frames be dismembered into separate member FBDs for analysis?

In ideal truss analysis, loads are assumed to be applied only at the _____, not along the members.

u1-L3-Q17 multiple-choice

What is the primary advantage of a Warren truss (no vertical members)?

u1-L3-Q18 multiple-choice

Neither joints nor sections alone can solve for a specific member. What approach?

u1-L3-Q19 true-false

If a truss member is found to have a negative force value when assumed to be in tension, the member is actually in compression.

u1-L3-Q20 multiple-choice

u1-L3-Q21 multiple-choice

u1-L3-Q25 true-false

In the method of joints, you should always solve for the external support reactions before beginning the joint-by-joint analysis.

u1-L3-Q26 multiple-choice

u1-L3-Q28 multiple-choice

What load assumption makes truss members two-force members?

A machine (as distinguished from a frame) is a structure designed to transmit and modify forces, and it always contains moving parts.

Belt Friction and Screws 10

u1-L4-Q21 multiple-choice

Why does r³ appear in the disc friction torque integral?

u1-L4-Q22 multiple-choice

Two blocks A (top) and B (bottom) are stacked on a horizontal surface. A horizontal force P is applied to block B. The friction coefficient between A and B is μ₁, and between B and the floor is μ₂. What is the maximum P for which both blocks move together?

u1-L4-Q23 fill-blank

A screw is _____-locking when its lead angle is less than the friction angle, preventing _____-driving under load.

u1-L4-Q24 multiple-choice

u1-L4-Q25 multiple-choice

10° wedge, 5000 N block, μ=0.2 all surfaces. Approximate driving force P?

u1-L4-Q26 true-false

In the Coulomb friction model, kinetic friction force is independent of the sliding velocity.

u1-L4-Q27 multiple-choice

to prevent a shaft from rotating under a torque of 150 N·m using a disc clutch. The clutch has inner radius 40 mm, outer radius 80 mm, and μ = 0.35. Using the uniform wear assumption, what axial clamping force is required?

u1-L4-Q28 multiple-choice

Why is a self-locking screw's efficiency always below 50%?

u1-L4-Q29 true-false

When analyzing a wedge problem, all friction forces must point in the same direction.

u1-L4-Q30 fill-blank

The capstan equation for belt friction is T₁/T₂ = e^(μθ), where θ must be expressed in _____.

Centroids of Simple Shapes 10

Why does centroid location matter for beam design?

the moment of inertia of a composite T-section by simply adding the centroidal moments of inertia of the flange and web without using the parallel axis theorem. What error will this introduce?

u1-L5-Q3 true-false

When computing the moment of inertia of a plate with a hole, you must apply the parallel axis theorem to BOTH the outer shape and the hole before subtracting, even if the hole is centered.

Which cross-section gives the best I-to-area ratio for minimum beam weight?

u1-L5-Q7 multiple-choice

What is the centroid (y-bar) of a right triangle with height h, measured from the base?

u1-L5-Q8 multiple-choice

The moment of inertia of a rectangle about its centroidal horizontal axis is bh³/12. What is it about the base (bottom edge)?

u1-L5-Q9 true-false

The moment of inertia (second moment of area) can be negative.

u1-L5-Q10 multiple-choice

Moment of Inertia 10

u1-L5-Q11 multiple-choice

What happens if you misapply the parallel axis theorem direction?

u1-L5-Q12 multiple-choice

What is the moment of inertia of a circle of diameter d about its centroidal axis?

u1-L5-Q13 true-false

The centroid of a composite area with a hole is found by treating the hole as a negative area in the weighted average formula.

u1-L5-Q14 multiple-choice

For a hollow circular tube with outer diameter D and inner diameter d, what is the area moment of inertia about a centroidal diameter?

u1-L5-Q15 multiple-choice

Why does a C-channel's displaced centroid matter for beam design?

u1-L5-Q16 fill-blank

u1-L5-Q17 multiple-choice

u1-L5-Q18 multiple-choice

What is the standard approach for finding the centroid of an L-shaped area?

u1-L5-Q19 true-false

The polar moment of inertia J = Ix + Iy for any planar shape, where Ix and Iy are the area moments of inertia about two perpendicular axes through the same point.

u1-L5-Q20 multiple-choice

A solid square cross-section has side length a. What is its moment of inertia about a centroidal axis parallel to one side?

Composite Sections 10

u1-L5-Q21 multiple-choice

u1-L5-Q22 multiple-choice

u1-L5-Q23 fill-blank

u1-L5-Q24 multiple-choice

When is the product of inertia Ixy important, and when does it vanish?

u1-L5-Q25 true-false

Doubling the depth of a rectangular beam cross-section while keeping the width constant increases the moment of inertia by a factor of 8.

u1-L5-Q26 multiple-choice

u1-L5-Q27 multiple-choice

u1-L5-Q28 multiple-choice

u1-L5-Q29 true-false

For the same cross-sectional area, a thin-walled hollow square tube has a higher moment of inertia than a solid square section.

Position, Velocity & Acceleration 7

At constant speed on a curve, does acceleration have both normal and tangential components?

Position x(t) = 3t³ - 2t² + 5t. What is the acceleration at t = 2 s?

u2-L1-Q9 true-false

If velocity is zero at an instant, must acceleration also be zero?

Curvilinear & Relative Motion 6

u2-L1-Q4 multiple-choice

u2-L1-Q6 fill-blank

The tangential acceleration of a particle in curvilinear motion equals the time rate of change of its _____.

u2-L1-Q5 multiple-choice

u2-L1-Q12 multiple-choice

Speed 20 m/s, radius 50 m, tangential acceleration 3 m/s². Total acceleration magnitude?

u2-L1-Q13 fill-blank

With air resistance, is the optimal launch angle for maximum range still 45°?

Projectiles & Advanced Kinematics 17

u2-L1-Q16 true-false

For a projectile launched on level ground (no air resistance), the time to reach maximum height equals exactly half the total flight time.

u2-L1-Q23 fill-blank

In projectile motion without air resistance, the horizontal component of velocity remains _____ throughout the flight.

u2-L1-Q11 multiple-choice

Car A: 60 km/h east. Car B: 80 km/h north. Velocity of B relative to A?

u2-L1-Q15 multiple-choice

The position of a particle is given in polar coordinates as r = 2 m (constant) and θ = 3t² rad. What is the magnitude of acceleration at t = 1 s?

u2-L1-Q17 multiple-choice

A particle starts from rest and moves along a straight line with acceleration a = 12 - 6t m/s² (t in seconds). At what time does the particle reach its maximum velocity?

u2-L1-Q18 multiple-choice

A wheel 0.6 m in diameter rotates at 300 rpm. What is the velocity of a point on the rim?

u2-L1-Q19 multiple-choice

A car traveling at 90 km/h applies brakes and decelerates uniformly to rest in 5 seconds. What is the braking distance?

u2-L1-Q20 true-false

The acceleration due to gravity on Earth is exactly constant regardless of altitude above the surface.

u2-L1-Q21 multiple-choice

A particle undergoes simple harmonic motion with amplitude 0.1 m and frequency 5 Hz. What is the maximum acceleration experienced by the particle?

u2-L1-Q22 multiple-choice

An engineer uses a position sensor to record x(t) data from a vibration test. To compute velocity, which method is more appropriate: numerical differentiation or curve fitting then analytical differentiation?

u2-L1-Q24 multiple-choice

A point on the rim of a rotating disk has a velocity of 10 m/s and a total acceleration of 500 m/s² directed at 30° from the radius. What is the angular acceleration of the disk?

u2-L1-Q25 multiple-choice

The velocity of a particle is given by v = (6t² - 2t + 4) m/s. What is the displacement during the interval from t = 1 s to t = 3 s?

u2-L1-Q26 true-false

A particle moving in a circle at increasing speed has an acceleration vector that points exactly toward the center of the circle.

u2-L1-Q27 multiple-choice

A helicopter pilot drops a package from 200 m altitude while flying horizontally at 50 m/s. How far ahead of the drop point does the package land?

u2-L1-Q28 multiple-choice

A particle moves along a path where the velocity is given as a function of position: v = sqrt(4s + 16) m/s (s in meters). What is the acceleration when s = 5 m?

u2-L1-Q29 multiple-choice

Two particles start at the origin. Particle A has velocity v_A = 3i + 4j m/s and Particle B has velocity v_B = -2i + 5j m/s. At what rate is the distance between them increasing at t = 2 s?

u2-L1-Q30 true-false

The equation v² = v₀² + 2a(s - s₀) is valid only for motion along a straight line with constant acceleration.

Newton's Laws Fundamentals 7

u2-L2-Q9 true-false

Newton's third law states that action and reaction forces act on the same body.

u2-L2-Q1 multiple-choice

Compared to pulling horizontally with the same force magnitude, what happens to the friction force?

u2-L2-Q2 multiple-choice

In an Atwood machine (two masses on a rope over a pulley), why is the acceleration always less than g, regardless of how different the masses are?

u2-L2-Q3 true-false

When a car goes over the top of a hill at speed v, the normal force from the road is less than the car's weight.

u2-L2-Q8 multiple-choice

Two blocks (A = 5 kg, B = 3 kg) are connected by a string and pulled across a frictionless surface by a force F = 40 N applied to block A. What is the tension in the string between the blocks?

u2-L2-Q7 multiple-choice

A 10 kg block sits on a 30° incline with static friction coefficient μ_s = 0.4. Is the block in equilibrium?

u2-L2-Q5 multiple-choice

You feel heavier when an elevator starts going up, and lighter when it starts going down. Using Newton's second law, explain what is actually changing.

Circular Motion & Banked Curves 8

u2-L2-Q16 true-false

On a banked curve at the ideal speed (no friction needed), the net force on the car is directed horizontally toward the center of the curve.

u2-L2-Q6 fill-blank

In normal-tangential coordinates for a particle on a curved path, the normal component of acceleration equals v²/_____.

u2-L2-Q4 multiple-choice

A highway exit ramp with radius 80 m is unbanked. The posted speed limit gives a comfortable margin on dry pavement (mu_s = 0.8). After an ice storm reduces friction to mu_s = 0.2, what should you reason about the safe speed?

u2-L2-Q10 multiple-choice

A 2000 kg car travels at 30 m/s around a banked curve with radius 150 m and banking angle 20°. If the road is frictionless, at what speed would the car travel the curve without any tendency to slide inward or outward?

u2-L2-Q12 multiple-choice

A 0.5 kg ball on a 1 m string swings in a vertical circle. What is the minimum speed at the top of the circle to maintain tension in the string?

u2-L2-Q14 multiple-choice

A conical pendulum (mass on a string tracing a horizontal circle) has string length L = 0.5 m and makes angle θ = 30° with vertical. What is the period of revolution?

u2-L2-Q22 multiple-choice

A washing machine spin cycle rotates clothes at 1200 rpm in a drum of radius 0.25 m. What is the centripetal acceleration in terms of g?

u2-L2-Q27 multiple-choice

design a flat (unbanked) circular test track for cars at 200 km/h. If the tire-road μ_s is 0.9, what is the minimum track radius?

Connected Systems & Real Problems 15

u2-L2-Q26 true-false

A body in equilibrium must have zero velocity.

u2-L2-Q13 fill-blank

The maximum static friction force on a body equals the coefficient of static friction multiplied by the _____ force.

u2-L2-Q11 multiple-choice

In a pulley system where a single rope loops around two pulleys to lift a load, the mechanical advantage is 2 but the rope must be pulled twice the distance. Why is this energy balance important?

u2-L2-Q15 multiple-choice

A truck accelerates at 3 m/s². A box sits on the truck bed with μ_s = 0.4. Does the box slide relative to the truck?

u2-L2-Q18 multiple-choice

Three blocks (m₁ = 2 kg, m₂ = 3 kg, m₃ = 5 kg) are stacked on a frictionless surface and pushed by a horizontal force F = 50 N applied to m₁. What is the contact force between m₂ and m₃?

u2-L2-Q20 true-false

In a tug-of-war, the team that pulls harder on the rope wins because they exert more force on the rope than the opposing team.

u2-L2-Q28 multiple-choice

In an Atwood machine with m₁ = 4 kg and m₂ = 6 kg (massless, frictionless pulley), what is the tension in the rope?

u2-L2-Q30 multiple-choice

A curved road has a superelevation (banking) angle of 8° and a design speed of 100 km/h. If the friction coefficient drops to zero (pure ice), what is the safe curve radius?

u2-L2-Q17 multiple-choice

A spring scale reads 80 N when a person stands on it in a stationary elevator. What does it read when the elevator accelerates downward at 2 m/s²?

u2-L2-Q19 multiple-choice

An engineer is designing a centrifuge to produce 500g of centripetal acceleration at the sample radius of 0.15 m. What rotational speed (rpm) is required?

u2-L2-Q21 multiple-choice

A 60 kg skier descends a 25° slope at constant velocity. What is the coefficient of kinetic friction between the skis and snow?

u2-L2-Q23 fill-blank

The condition for a block to remain stationary on an incline without any applied force is that the incline angle must be less than arctan(_____).

u2-L2-Q24 multiple-choice

A block of mass m hangs from two ropes making angles of 30° and 60° with the horizontal ceiling. Which rope has higher tension?

u2-L2-Q25 multiple-choice

A package on a conveyor belt accelerates from rest. The belt surface has μ_s = 0.3 and μ_k = 0.2. What is the maximum belt acceleration before the package begins to slip?

u2-L2-Q29 fill-blank

The apparent weight of a person in an elevator equals m(g + a) when the elevator accelerates _____.

Work & Kinetic Energy 7

u2-L3-Q3 true-false

The work-energy theorem states that the net work done on a particle equals its change in kinetic energy, regardless of the type of forces involved.

u2-L3-Q9 true-false

A force that is always perpendicular to the velocity of a particle does zero work on that particle.

u2-L3-Q1 multiple-choice

When would you prefer to use the work-energy method instead of Newton's second law (F=ma) to solve a dynamics problem?

u2-L3-Q6 fill-blank

The work done by friction is always _____ because friction acts opposite to the direction of _____.

u2-L3-Q7 multiple-choice

A 1200 kg car traveling at 100 km/h brakes to a stop. How much energy must the brakes absorb?

u2-L3-Q10 multiple-choice

A 50 kg box is pushed 10 m across a floor (μ_k = 0.3) by a horizontal 200 N force. What is the net work done on the box?

u2-L3-Q15 multiple-choice

A force F = 3x² N (where x is in meters) acts on a particle along the x-axis. How much work does this force do as the particle moves from x = 1 m to x = 4 m?

Conservation of Energy 8

u2-L3-Q5 multiple-choice

A roller coaster car starts from rest at the top of a 30 m hill and reaches the bottom where it enters a loop. Using energy conservation, why does the car's mass not affect its speed at the bottom (neglecting friction)?

u2-L3-Q16 true-false

Gravitational potential energy is always positive.

u2-L3-Q2 multiple-choice

A spring-loaded mechanism uses a spring compressed 5 mm. The designer wants to double the stored energy. What are the two options, and which is more practical?

u2-L3-Q8 multiple-choice

A spring with k = 500 N/m is compressed 0.1 m from its natural length. If the spring pushes a 0.2 kg ball, what speed does the ball reach (assuming all energy transfers to the ball)?

u2-L3-Q12 multiple-choice

A 2 kg block slides down a 5 m long frictionless ramp starting from rest. The ramp height is 3 m. What is the block's speed at the bottom?

u2-L3-Q14 multiple-choice

A child on a swing reaches a maximum height of 1.5 m above the lowest point. What is the child's speed at the lowest point, neglecting air resistance?

u2-L3-Q24 multiple-choice

A 0.5 kg ball is dropped from 10 m height onto a spring (k = 2000 N/m). What is the maximum compression of the spring?

u2-L3-Q28 multiple-choice

A 10 kg block slides 8 m down a rough incline (30°, μ_k = 0.25) starting from rest. What is the speed at the bottom?

Power & Efficiency 15

u2-L3-Q13 fill-blank

Power is defined as the rate of doing work, and for a constant force moving an object at constant velocity, P = F times _____.

u2-L3-Q20 true-false

If the total mechanical energy of a system is conserved, no non-conservative forces (like friction) are doing work on the system.

u2-L3-Q4 multiple-choice

A motor lifts a 500 kg load at a constant velocity of 2 m/s, requiring P = mg*v = 9,810 W of ideal power. The motor is rated at 15 kW. Is the motor adequately sized, and what practical factors should you consider?

u2-L3-Q11 multiple-choice

A pump raises water through a height of 20 m at a flow rate of 0.05 m³/s. What power is required? (Water density = 1000 kg/m³)

u2-L3-Q19 multiple-choice

A car engine produces 120 kW at 4000 rpm. What is the engine torque at this operating point?

u2-L3-Q25 multiple-choice

A 75 kW engine drives a car at constant 120 km/h on a level road. What is the total resistance force (air drag + rolling resistance)?

u2-L3-Q29 multiple-choice

The efficiency of a machine is 60%. If the useful output work is 300 J, how much input energy was required?

u2-L3-Q30 true-false

If a car doubles its speed, the engine must deliver four times the power to overcome the same drag force.

u2-L3-Q17 multiple-choice

An elevator motor must accelerate a 1000 kg car (including passengers) from rest to 3 m/s while lifting it 5 m. What is the minimum average power if this takes 4 seconds?

u2-L3-Q18 multiple-choice

Two springs in series (both k = 1000 N/m) support a 10 kg mass. What is the total elastic potential energy stored when the system is in static equilibrium?

u2-L3-Q21 multiple-choice

A bungee jumper (80 kg) jumps from a 50 m bridge. The bungee cord has natural length 10 m and k = 100 N/m. What is the maximum distance the jumper falls?

u2-L3-Q22 multiple-choice

Two identical cars, one traveling at 30 km/h and another at 90 km/h, both brake to a stop. How do their braking distances compare (same braking force)?

u2-L3-Q23 fill-blank

A conservative force is one for which the work done depends only on the initial and final positions, not on the _____ taken.

u2-L3-Q26 true-false

The work done by the normal force on a block sliding down an inclined plane is zero.

u2-L3-Q27 multiple-choice

A 500 kg satellite orbits Earth at 300 km altitude. What type of energy (kinetic, potential, or both) changes if the satellite moves to a higher orbit?

Momentum & Impulse Basics 8

u2-L4-Q3 true-false

In an elastic collision, both kinetic energy and momentum are conserved.

u2-L4-Q9 true-false

In a perfectly inelastic collision, the objects always stick together and move with a common velocity after impact.

u2-L4-Q1 multiple-choice

A car crumple zone is designed to increase the duration of a crash. Using the impulse-momentum theorem, explain why this reduces injury risk

u2-L4-Q2 multiple-choice

A 0.01 kg bullet traveling at 400 m/s embeds in a 2 kg wooden block on a frictionless surface. What is the block's velocity after impact?

u2-L4-Q4 multiple-choice

During a collision, momentum is always conserved but kinetic energy may not be. What physically happens to the "lost" kinetic energy in a real collision?

u2-L4-Q5 multiple-choice

In a two-car head-on collision, Car A (1500 kg, 20 m/s east) strikes Car B (1000 kg, 15 m/s west). An accident investigator needs to determine the system's motion immediately after impact. Which conservation law should they apply first?

u2-L4-Q7 multiple-choice

A 2 kg ball moving at 6 m/s collides head-on with a 4 kg ball at rest. If the collision is perfectly elastic, what are the final velocities?

u2-L4-Q8 multiple-choice

The coefficient of restitution for a collision between a ball and the floor is 0.8. If the ball is dropped from 2 m height, to what height does it bounce?

Collisions & Restitution 8

u2-L4-Q16 true-false

During a collision between two objects, the impulse on object A from object B is equal in magnitude but opposite in direction to the impulse on object B from object A.

u2-L4-Q13 fill-blank

The _____ delivered to an object equals the area under the force versus _____ curve.

u2-L4-Q10 multiple-choice

A 5 kg object at rest explodes into two pieces: a 2 kg piece and a 3 kg piece. If the 2 kg piece flies off at 15 m/s, what is the speed of the 3 kg piece?

u2-L4-Q11 multiple-choice

A water jet hits a stationary flat plate perpendicularly at 20 m/s with a mass flow rate of 5 kg/s. What force does the jet exert on the plate (assuming the water does not bounce back)?

u2-L4-Q12 multiple-choice

A 70 kg hockey player moving at 8 m/s collides with a stationary 90 kg player. They grab each other and slide together. What percentage of kinetic energy is lost?

u2-L4-Q14 multiple-choice

A ball of mass m moving at velocity v strikes a wall and rebounds with the same speed. What is the impulse exerted by the wall on the ball?

u2-L4-Q15 multiple-choice

A 1000 kg rocket in space ejects exhaust at 3000 m/s relative to the rocket. To achieve a velocity change of 600 m/s, approximately what fraction of the initial mass must be fuel?

u2-L4-Q6 fill-blank

In a collision between two objects with no external forces, the total _____ of the system is conserved, even if kinetic energy is not.

Momentum in Systems & Machines 14

u2-L4-Q20 true-false

Momentum is a scalar quantity, like energy.

u2-L4-Q23 fill-blank

The coefficient of restitution e for a perfectly elastic collision equals _____.

u2-L4-Q17 multiple-choice

A baseball (0.145 kg) is pitched at 40 m/s and hit back at 50 m/s. If the bat-ball contact lasts 1 ms, what is the average force on the ball?

u2-L4-Q18 multiple-choice

In a 2D collision, a ball moving at 10 m/s strikes a stationary ball of equal mass at a glancing angle. After the elastic collision, the two balls move at 90° to each other. Why does this 90° result occur?

u2-L4-Q19 multiple-choice

A sand hopper drops sand vertically onto a conveyor belt moving at 3 m/s at a rate of 20 kg/s. What horizontal force must the belt motor provide to keep the belt at constant speed?

u2-L4-Q21 multiple-choice

Two objects collide with a coefficient of restitution e = 0.6. Object 1 (3 kg) moves at 8 m/s and Object 2 (5 kg) moves at -4 m/s (head-on). What is the relative velocity of separation?

u2-L4-Q22 multiple-choice

A firefighter directs a hose with flow rate 0.02 m³/s and nozzle exit velocity 30 m/s at a wall. What thrust force does the firefighter feel from the hose reaction?

u2-L4-Q24 multiple-choice

A 50 kg person standing on a 100 kg boat (initially at rest on frictionless water) walks 3 m toward the bow. How far does the boat move in the opposite direction?

u2-L4-Q25 multiple-choice

In a ballistic pendulum, a bullet embeds in a suspended block which swings upward. Why is momentum conservation used for the collision but energy conservation used for the swing?

u2-L4-Q26 true-false

A system can have zero total momentum but nonzero total kinetic energy.

u2-L4-Q27 multiple-choice

A 3 kg ball hits a 1 kg ball at rest in a perfectly elastic head-on collision. What fraction of the 3 kg ball's kinetic energy is transferred to the 1 kg ball?

u2-L4-Q28 multiple-choice

A force of F(t) = 100(1 - t/0.01) N acts during a 0.01 s impact (triangular pulse starting at 100 N and decreasing linearly to zero). What is the total impulse?

u2-L4-Q29 fill-blank

When no external forces act on a system, the velocity of the center of _____ remains constant.

u2-L4-Q30 multiple-choice

A machine gun fires 10 bullets per second, each bullet having mass 0.03 kg and muzzle velocity 800 m/s. What average force must the shooter exert to hold the gun steady?

Torque & Moment of Inertia 8

u2-L5-Q3 true-false

For a wheel rolling without slipping, the velocity at the contact point with the ground is zero.

u2-L5-Q9 true-false

The mass moment of inertia of a body depends on the axis of rotation chosen.

u2-L5-Q1 multiple-choice

A flywheel designer needs to maximize energy storage for a given mass. How should they distribute the mass, and what formula justifies this?

u2-L5-Q2 multiple-choice

A solid sphere and a hollow sphere of the same mass and radius roll down the same incline without slipping. Which reaches the bottom first?

u2-L5-Q4 multiple-choice

A flywheel spins at 300 rpm and stores about 987 J of rotational kinetic energy. If the speed is doubled to 600 rpm, how much energy does it store?

u2-L5-Q5 multiple-choice

A spinning bicycle wheel held by one end of its axle does not fall but instead precesses slowly. A student asks "Why does spinning faster make it precess slower?

u2-L5-Q7 multiple-choice

The moment of inertia of a solid cylinder about its central axis is ½mR². Using the parallel axis theorem, what is the moment of inertia about an axis parallel to the central axis but located at the rim?

u2-L5-Q6 fill-blank

For pure rolling without slipping, the contact point is instantaneously at _____, requiring the constraint v = _____.

Angular Momentum & Gyroscopes 8

u2-L5-Q16 true-false

The angular momentum of a system is conserved only when no external forces act on the system.

u2-L5-Q13 fill-blank

The rotational analog of F = ma is τ = I × _____.

u2-L5-Q8 multiple-choice

A figure skater pulls in her arms during a spin and her angular velocity triples. By what factor did her moment of inertia change?

u2-L5-Q10 multiple-choice

A uniform rod of mass 4 kg and length 1.2 m is pivoted at one end. What angular acceleration results from a 10 N force applied perpendicular to the rod at the other end?

u2-L5-Q11 multiple-choice

A solid disk (I = ½mR²) and a thin ring (I = mR²) of the same mass and radius roll down an incline. What is the ratio of their translational accelerations?

u2-L5-Q12 multiple-choice

A 20 kg solid disk of radius 0.3 m has a rope wrapped around it. A 5 kg mass hangs from the rope. What is the angular acceleration of the disk?

u2-L5-Q14 multiple-choice

Two gears mesh together. Gear A has 40 teeth and Gear B has 20 teeth. If Gear A rotates at 100 rpm, what is Gear B's speed and which direction does it rotate?

u2-L5-Q15 multiple-choice

A 50 kg child sits at the rim of a 200 kg merry-go-round (uniform disk, R = 2 m) spinning at 10 rpm. The child walks to the center. What is the new angular velocity?

Rolling & Rotational Applications 14

u2-L5-Q20 true-false

A hollow cylinder has a larger moment of inertia than a solid cylinder of the same mass and outer radius.

u2-L5-Q23 fill-blank

The moment of inertia of a thin rod of mass m and length L about an axis through its center, perpendicular to its length, is (1/12)mL². About one end, it is (1/_____)mL².

u2-L5-Q17 multiple-choice

A wheel of radius 0.4 m rolling without slipping has a center velocity of 5 m/s. What is the velocity of the topmost point of the wheel relative to the ground?

u2-L5-Q18 multiple-choice

The perpendicular axis theorem states that for a planar body, I_z = I_x + I_y. This theorem applies to which type of body?

u2-L5-Q19 multiple-choice

A solid cylinder rolls up an incline without slipping, starting at 4 m/s. How high does it climb before momentarily stopping?

u2-L5-Q21 multiple-choice

A torque of 50 N·m is applied to a flywheel with I = 10 kg·m² initially at rest. How many revolutions does it complete in 4 seconds?

u2-L5-Q22 multiple-choice

In a car differential, the inner wheel turns slower and the outer wheel turns faster than the driveshaft during a turn. What principle allows this?

u2-L5-Q24 multiple-choice

A student places a spinning gyroscope on a table with its axis horizontal. Instead of tipping over, the axis rotates horizontally. If the gyroscope spin rate is 3000 rpm, I_spin = 0.001 kg·m², and the center of mass is 0.05 m from the support point with mass 0.5 kg, what is the precession rate?

u2-L5-Q25 multiple-choice

Why is it easier to balance on a moving bicycle than a stationary one?

u2-L5-Q26 true-false

When a solid ball rolls down an incline without slipping, friction does negative work on the ball.

u2-L5-Q27 multiple-choice

A turntable (I = 0.5 kg·m²) rotates at 33.3 rpm. A 0.3 kg record (I_record = ½mR² = 0.024 kg·m²) is dropped onto it from rest. What is the final angular velocity?

u2-L5-Q28 multiple-choice

A composite body consists of a solid disk (m = 5 kg, R = 0.2 m) with a point mass of 2 kg attached at its rim. What is the total moment of inertia about the disk's central axis?

u2-L5-Q29 multiple-choice

A thin uniform rod of mass 3 kg and length 0.8 m is pivoted at one end and released from a horizontal position. What is the angular velocity when it reaches the vertical position?

u2-L5-Q30 fill-blank

The radius of gyration k relates the moment of inertia and mass by I = m × _____.

Natural Frequency & Free Vibration 8

u2-L6-Q3 true-false

At resonance in a lightly damped forced-vibration system, the steady-state response amplitude is limited only by the damping. Without damping, the amplitude would grow without bound.

u2-L6-Q9 true-false

A critically damped system (ζ = 1) oscillates exactly once before coming to rest.

u2-L6-Q1 multiple-choice

A machine on its mounting vibrates excessively. You want to lower the natural frequency to avoid resonance. Which approach works?

u2-L6-Q2 multiple-choice

For a door closer mechanism, would you want underdamped, critically damped, or overdamped behavior?

u2-L6-Q4 multiple-choice

A machine on soft isolators (r = omega/omega_n = 6.3) transmits only 2.6% of its vibration force to the floor. A technician suggests adding stiff rubber bumpers for "extra protection." Why could this actually make things worse?

u2-L6-Q5 multiple-choice

A rotating shaft balanced at 1500 rpm develops excessive vibration when the speed is increased to 3000 rpm. The operator says "But we balanced it!" Why is the vibration so much worse at the higher speed?

u2-L6-Q6 fill-blank

When the forcing frequency equals the natural frequency, the system is in _____, and the response amplitude is limited only by damping.

u2-L6-Q7 multiple-choice

A spring-mass system has k = 4000 N/m and m = 10 kg. What is the natural frequency in Hz?

Forced Vibration & Resonance 8

u2-L6-Q16 true-false

The damped natural frequency ω_d is always less than the undamped natural frequency ω_n.

u2-L6-Q13 fill-blank

When ζ < 1 the system is _____, and when ζ = 1 it is called _____ damped.

u2-L6-Q8 multiple-choice

A vibrating system has a damping ratio ζ = 0.1. What is the amplification factor (Q factor) at resonance?

u2-L6-Q10 multiple-choice

An underdamped system with ζ = 0.05 and ω_n = 50 rad/s undergoes free vibration. Approximately how many cycles does it take for the amplitude to decay to half its initial value?

u2-L6-Q11 multiple-choice

The logarithmic decrement δ is measured from a free vibration test as 0.314. What is the damping ratio ζ?

u2-L6-Q12 multiple-choice

A vibration isolator must reduce transmitted force by 80% (transmissibility = 0.2). If the machine operates at 1200 rpm, what is the maximum allowable natural frequency of the isolator mounting?

u2-L6-Q14 multiple-choice

A 100 kg machine is mounted on springs with total stiffness 40,000 N/m and operates at 600 rpm. Is the machine in the isolation range?

u2-L6-Q15 multiple-choice

Why does adding damping to a vibration isolator actually INCREASE the transmitted force at high frequency ratios (r >> 1)?

Vibration Isolation & Absorbers 14

u2-L6-Q20 true-false

Doubling the mass of a vibrating system while keeping stiffness constant will halve the natural frequency.

u2-L6-Q23 fill-blank

The period of free vibration of an undamped spring-mass system is T = 2π × sqrt(m/_____) .

u2-L6-Q17 multiple-choice

A car suspension system has a natural frequency of 1 Hz and a damping ratio of 0.3. After hitting a bump, approximately how many cycles of oscillation occur before the motion becomes imperceptible (< 2% of initial amplitude)?

u2-L6-Q18 multiple-choice

A simple pendulum has length L = 1 m. What is its natural frequency of small oscillations?

u2-L6-Q19 multiple-choice

An undamped absorber (tuned mass damper) is added to a structure to suppress vibration at a specific frequency. What must the absorber's natural frequency equal?

u2-L6-Q21 multiple-choice

A machine creates a harmonic force of 500 N at 30 Hz. If the machine is mounted on isolators with transmissibility 0.15, what force reaches the foundation?

u2-L6-Q22 multiple-choice

Springs in parallel under a machine have what effect compared to a single spring of the same total stiffness?

u2-L6-Q24 multiple-choice

A structure has a measured natural frequency of 5 Hz. An earthquake excites it at 5 Hz for 10 seconds. If ζ = 0.02, approximately how large could the dynamic amplification become during this excitation?

u2-L6-Q25 multiple-choice

A rotating machine has an unbalance of 0.001 kg·m. At what speed in rpm would the unbalance force equal 100 N?

u2-L6-Q26 true-false

The phase angle between the forcing function and the steady-state response of a damped SDOF system at resonance is exactly 90 degrees.

u2-L6-Q27 multiple-choice

A vibration test reveals that a machine mount deflects 2 mm under static load (dead weight). What is the natural frequency of vertical vibration?

u2-L6-Q28 multiple-choice

A lightly damped system (ζ = 0.02) operates at r = 0.95 (just below resonance). What is the approximate dynamic magnification factor?

u2-L6-Q29 fill-blank

For an undamped system, vibration isolation begins when r = ω/ω_n exceeds _____; below this value the mount causes _____.

u2-L6-Q30 multiple-choice

A Frahm absorber (undamped dynamic vibration absorber) eliminates vibration at ONE frequency but creates TWO new resonance frequencies. Why is this a potential concern?

💪

Strength of Materials

210 diagrams across 21 lessons

Stress & Strain Fundamentals 9

u3-L1-Q1 multiple-choice

Same load and dimensions on aluminum vs. steel rods. How do stress and strain compare?

u3-L1-Q2 multiple-choice

Why does rubber thin visibly under tension but steel barely thins?

u3-L1-Q3 true-false

On an engineering stress-strain curve, stress drops after UTS. Is the material actually weakening?

u3-L1-Q4 multiple-choice

Why is the 0.2% offset method used to define yield strength?

u3-L1-Q5 multiple-choice

Steel rod: L=2m, E=200GPa, A=100mm², F=20kN. Elongation?

u3-L1-Q6 fill-blank

Reversible deformation below yield is _____, permanent above yield is _____.

u3-L1-Q7 multiple-choice

Tapered bar (200 mm² at A, 400 mm² at B) carries 40 kN. Where is stress maximum?

u3-L1-Q8 multiple-choice

Gauge length goes from 50 mm to 62 mm after fracture. Percent elongation?

u3-L1-Q9 multiple-choice

G = E/(2(1+nu)). For steel (E=200 GPa, nu=0.3), what is G?

Stress & Strain Fundamentals II 10

u3-L1-Q10 true-false

Engineering and true strain are nearly identical below ~5% strain.

u3-L1-Q11 multiple-choice

Steel core (500mm², 200GPa) bonded to Al sleeve (800mm², 70GPa), 100kN. Steel's load fraction?

u3-L1-Q12 multiple-choice

Restrained steel bolt heated 50°C (alpha=12e-6/°C, E=200GPa). Thermal stress?

u3-L1-Q13 multiple-choice

What does the area under the stress-strain curve represent?

u3-L1-Q14 true-false

Higher UTS always means higher toughness.

u3-L1-Q15 multiple-choice

10mm pin in double shear, 15kN total. Shear stress?

u3-L1-Q16 multiple-choice

How do proportional limit, elastic limit, and yield strength differ?

u3-L1-Q17 multiple-choice

E=200 GPa, G=80 GPa. Poisson's ratio?

u3-L1-Q18 true-false

Poisson's ratio can range from -1.0 to 0.5, and auxetic materials exist.

u3-L1-Q19 multiple-choice

In mild steel's tensile test, the upper yield point is higher than the lower yield point. What causes this phenomenon?

Stress & Strain Fundamentals III 11

u3-L1-Q20 multiple-choice

Kt=1.8 at a fillet, nominal stress=80 MPa. Maximum stress?

u3-L1-Q21 multiple-choice

When does compressive behavior of ductile metals diverge from tensile?

u3-L1-Q22 fill-blank

The ratio of lateral to axial strain is called _____ ratio.

u3-L1-Q23 multiple-choice

Copper (E=120, Sy=70) vs steel (E=200, Sy=250). Which stores more elastic energy per volume?

u3-L1-Q24 true-false

Strain is dimensionless because it is deltaL/L_0.

u3-L1-Q25 multiple-choice

How is bearing stress calculated for a bolt through a plate?

u3-L1-Q26 multiple-choice

How does stress vary in a bar hanging under its own weight?

u3-L1-Q27 true-false

K = E/(3(1-2nu)) approaches infinity as nu approaches 0.5 (incompressibility).

u3-L1-Q28 multiple-choice

10mm bolt preloaded to 20kN, SAE Grade 8 (proof=830MPa). Adequate?

u3-L1-Q29 multiple-choice

Double both L and A of a bar, same load. What happens to elongation?

u3-L1-Q30 fill-blank

The linear stress-strain slope is the modulus of _____ (Young's modulus).

Beam Bending 9

u3-L2-Q1 multiple-choice

Beam deflects too much but stress is fine. How to reduce deflection?

u3-L2-Q2 multiple-choice

What is the practical consequence of a T-beam's asymmetric NA?

u3-L2-Q3 true-false

In pure bending, the neutral axis passes through the centroid.

u3-L2-Q4 multiple-choice

Rotating a 50x100mm beam to 100x50mm. How do bending strength and stiffness change?

u3-L2-Q5 multiple-choice

delta scales with L^4. To double span at same deflection, how must EI change?

u3-L2-Q6 fill-blank

S = _____/c simplifies bending stress to sigma_max = M/_____.

u3-L2-Q7 multiple-choice

SS beam, center load P, rectangular b x h. Maximum bending stress?

u3-L2-Q8 multiple-choice

Same area: solid rectangle vs I-beam. Which is stiffer in bending?

u3-L2-Q9 true-false

Doubling depth doubles both bending strength and stiffness of a rectangular beam.

Beam Bending II 11

u3-L2-Q10 multiple-choice

Cantilever with tip load P. Where is M_max and its value?

u3-L2-Q11 multiple-choice

In sigma = My/I, what does the variable y represent, and what is its sign convention?

u3-L2-Q12 multiple-choice

SS beam: L=4m, w=10kN/m, E=200GPa, I=8.33x10^6 mm^4. Max deflection?

u3-L2-Q13 true-false

The flexure formula sigma = My/I is valid for any beam.

u3-L2-Q14 multiple-choice

Why are I-beams oriented with web vertical in buildings?

u3-L2-Q15 multiple-choice

M=50 kN*m, sigma_allow=160 MPa. Minimum section modulus?

u3-L2-Q16 multiple-choice

Wood (E=12GPa) + Al plate (E=70GPa). How to use transformed section method?

u3-L2-Q17 multiple-choice

Shear stress distribution across a rectangular beam section?

u3-L2-Q18 true-false

For long beams, bending stress is more critical than shear stress.

u3-L2-Q19 multiple-choice

Column Buckling & Pressure Vessels 9

u3-L7-Q1 multiple-choice

Column buckles below yield. Will higher-strength steel help?

u3-L7-Q2 multiple-choice

A flagpole (fixed base, free top) and a column in a braced building frame (pinned-pinned) have the same length and cross-section. The flagpole buckles at only 1/4 of the building column's buckling load. Why?

u3-L7-Q3 multiple-choice

When is Euler's buckling formula NOT valid, and what should you use instead?

u3-L7-Q4 multiple-choice

The hoop stress is exactly twice the axial stress. What practical consequence does this have for how pressure vessels fail?

u3-L7-Q5 true-false

Simply increasing wall thickness is always an effective way to increase the pressure capacity of a thick-walled pressure vessel.

u3-L7-Q6 fill-blank

Hoop stress sigma_h = pD/(2t) and axial stress sigma_a = pD/(4t). The ratio of hoop to axial stress is always _____.

u3-L7-Q7 multiple-choice

A steel column (E = 200 GPa, Sy = 250 MPa) has a slenderness ratio KL/r = 120. Is Euler's formula valid?

u3-L7-Q8 multiple-choice

A rectangular cross-section 30 mm x 60 mm and length 2 m with pinned-pinned ends. About which axis will it buckle?

u3-L7-Q9 true-false

Adding lateral bracing at the midpoint of a pinned-pinned column effectively doubles its buckling load.

Column Buckling & Pressure Vessels II 11

u3-L7-Q10 multiple-choice

A thin-walled cylindrical pressure vessel has diameter D = 1 m, wall thickness t = 10 mm, and internal pressure p = 2 MPa. What are the hoop and axial stresses?

u3-L7-Q11 multiple-choice

A spherical pressure vessel and a cylindrical pressure vessel have the same diameter and wall thickness under the same pressure. How do their stress states compare?

u3-L7-Q12 multiple-choice

Johnson's parabolic formula is sigma_cr = Sy - (Sy²/(4*pi²*E))*(KL/r)². What is the physical interpretation of this formula?

u3-L7-Q13 true-false

The thin-wall pressure vessel formula sigma = pD/(2t) is valid when the ratio of diameter to wall thickness (D/t) is greater than approximately 20.

u3-L7-Q14 multiple-choice

What is the radius of gyration r, and why is it important for column design?

u3-L7-Q15 multiple-choice

A thick-walled cylinder has inner radius r_i = 50 mm, outer radius r_o = 100 mm, and internal pressure p = 100 MPa. What is the maximum hoop stress using the Lame equation?

u3-L7-Q16 multiple-choice

What is the effective length factor K for a column with one end fixed and the other end pinned?

u3-L7-Q17 fill-blank

The Euler critical load formula is P_cr = pi²EI/(KL)². The quantity KL is called the _____ length of the column.

u3-L7-Q18 multiple-choice

A W-shape cross-section. The strong-axis moment of inertia is 5 times the weak-axis value (Ix = 5*Iy). If the column is braced about the weak axis at midheight but unbraced about the strong axis, which axis governs buckling?

u3-L7-Q19 multiple-choice

What causes a hot dog to split lengthwise when overheated, and how does this relate to pressure vessel design?

u3-L7-Q20 true-false

Eccentric loading on a column (load applied off-center) always reduces the buckling capacity compared to concentric loading.

Column Buckling & Pressure Vessels III 10

u3-L7-Q21 multiple-choice

For a pressure vessel operating under both internal pressure and external loads (weight, wind, seismic), what additional design considerations arise?

u3-L7-Q22 multiple-choice

What is autofrettage, and why is it used for high-pressure cylinders (gun barrels, hydraulic systems)?

u3-L7-Q23 fill-blank

The slenderness ratio of a column is defined as KL/_____, where the denominator is the minimum radius of gyration.

u3-L7-Q24 multiple-choice

A steel pipe column (D = 150 mm, t = 5 mm, L = 4 m, pinned-pinned) is to be used as a structural column. What is the approximate Euler buckling load?

u3-L7-Q25 multiple-choice

What is a compound cylinder (shrink-fit cylinder), and why is it used instead of a single thick cylinder?

u3-L7-Q26 true-false

For a thin-walled cylindrical pressure vessel under internal pressure only, the von Mises stress equals sigma_h * sqrt(3)/2, where sigma_h is the hoop stress.

u3-L7-Q27 multiple-choice

An engineer must design a column to carry 500 kN with a 3 m height. Which cross-section shape would be most efficient (highest P_cr for minimum weight)?

u3-L7-Q28 multiple-choice

In the design of pressure vessel heads (end caps), what are the common types and their relative advantages?

u3-L7-Q29 multiple-choice

External pressure on a thin-walled cylinder (like a submarine or vacuum vessel) causes a different failure mode than internal pressure. What is it?

u3-L7-Q30 true-false

Cross-section, and length, changing from fixed-fixed (K=0.5) to pinned-pinned (K=1.0) end conditions reduces the Euler buckling load to one-quarter of the fixed-fixed value.

Thermodynamics

106 diagrams across 15 lessons

Properties & State: Basics 6

The Gibbs phase rule for a non-reacting system is F = C - P + 2, where C is the number of components and P is the number of phases.

Properties & State: Phases 8

First Law: Foundations 7

u4-L2-Q1 multiple-choice

u4-L2-Q12 true-false

Boundary work for a constant-volume process is always zero.

u4-L2-Q21 true-false

Work done by a gas during expansion depends on the process path, not just the initial and final states.

First Law: Open Systems 9

u4-L2-Q9 true-false

In a steady-state open system, the total energy and mass within the control volume stay constant over time.

u4-L2-Q2 multiple-choice

u4-L2-Q3 true-false

A throttling valve is modeled as isenthalpic (h_in = h_out) because it has no work, negligible heat transfer, and small KE change.

u4-L2-Q11 multiple-choice

u4-L2-Q14 multiple-choice

u4-L2-Q23 multiple-choice

u4-L2-Q17 multiple-choice

u4-L2-Q26 true-false

Power Cycles: Rankine 6

u4-L4-Q1 multiple-choice

Why does the Rankine cycle have such a low back-work ratio (1-3%)?

u4-L4-Q2 multiple-choice

How does reheating improve the Rankine cycle?

u4-L4-Q3 true-false

Regeneration in Rankine uses extracted steam to preheat feedwater before the boiler.

u4-L4-Q4 multiple-choice

Why must turbine exit quality stay above about 90%?

u4-L4-Q5 multiple-choice

Ideal Rankine: Q_in=2500, Q_out=1800 kJ/kg. Thermal efficiency?

u4-L4-Q6 true-false

In a supercritical Rankine cycle, there is no distinct boiling process.

Power Cycles: Gas Engines 7

u4-L4-Q7 multiple-choice

Ideal Otto efficiency with r=8, gamma=1.4?

u4-L4-Q8 multiple-choice

At the same compression ratio, which has higher efficiency?

u4-L4-Q9 true-false

The Brayton cycle (gas turbine) has a much higher BWR than the Rankine cycle.

u4-L4-Q10 multiple-choice

How does increasing pressure ratio affect ideal Brayton efficiency?

u4-L4-Q12 true-false

In the ideal Brayton cycle, thermal efficiency depends only on pressure ratio, not on T_max.

u4-L4-Q13 multiple-choice

Main advantage of a combined Brayton-Rankine cycle?

What is the dual cycle?

Mean effective pressure (MEP) is:

u4-L4-Q15 fill-blank

The Brayton cycle uses _____ as the working fluid.

u4-L4-Q16 multiple-choice

Brayton regeneration works by:

u4-L4-Q18 true-false

The Ericsson cycle with perfect regeneration achieves Carnot efficiency.

u4-L4-Q19 multiple-choice

Combined cycle: gas turbine 38% efficient, steam captures 30% of rejected heat. Overall?

u4-L4-Q20 multiple-choice

Why can't we just raise the compression ratio in Otto engines?

u4-L4-Q21 true-false

Increasing turbine inlet temperature is the most effective way to improve Brayton specific work output.

The 4 components of a basic vapor-compression refrigeration cycle:

u4-L5-Q2 multiple-choice

A heat pump with COP=4 delivers 4 kW of heating for every 1 kW of electricity.

Superheating at evaporator exit ensures refrigerant entering the _____ is completely vapor.

Why is subcooling before the expansion valve beneficial?

u4-L5-Q8 multiple-choice

u4-L5-Q9 true-false

Absorption refrigeration replaces the compressor with a heat-driven absorber-generator pair.

What happens if the condenser is dirty and partially blocked?

u4-L5-Q11 multiple-choice

What does a suction line accumulator do?

u4-L5-Q12 true-false

A thermoelectric (Peltier) cooler uses refrigerant and a compressor.

During a heat pump defrost cycle:

SEER stands for _____ Energy Efficiency Ratio.

An oversized AC is worse than a properly sized one because it short-cycles and fails to dehumidify.

Geothermal heat pumps have higher COP than air-source because:

What is wrong with this reasoning?

The buoyant force on a fully submerged object depends on the object's weight and density.

u6-L1-Q10 multiple-choice

What is the resultant hydrostatic force on the gate? (rho_water = 1000 kg/m3)

u6-L1-Q11 multiple-choice

In a differential manometer, if both legs contain the same fluid and no manometer fluid is used, the manometer cannot measure a pressure difference.

u6-L2-Q1 multiple-choice

A pitot tube can accurately measure air velocity in a ventilation duct regardless of whether the flow is laminar or turbulent.
