How Things Work

140 diagrams across 7 lessons

Kitchen Engineering 20

htw-L1-Q1 multiple-choice

A pressure cooker cooks food faster primarily because:

htw-L1-Q2 fill-blank

The _____ relation describes how the boiling point of a liquid changes with pressure, which is the principle behind a pressure cooker.

htw-L1-Q3 multiple-choice

A cylindrical pressure cooker wall is under internal pressure. The hoop (circumferential) stress in the wall is:

htw-L1-Q4 true-false

The safety valve on a pressure cooker is a dead-weight relief valve — it lifts when internal pressure exceeds the force of a calibrated weight divided by the valve seat area.

htw-L1-Q5 multiple-choice

In a household refrigerator, the evaporator is located:

htw-L1-Q6 multiple-choice

A refrigerator has a COP of 4. This means:

htw-L1-Q7 fill-blank

In the vapor-compression refrigeration cycle, the _____ reduces refrigerant pressure without extracting work, causing a temperature drop via throttling.

htw-L1-Q8 true-false

In a refrigerator's vapor-compression cycle, the refrigerant enters the compressor as a superheated vapor and leaves the condenser as a subcooled liquid.

htw-L1-Q9 multiple-choice

A kitchen faucet aerator mixes air into the water stream. From a fluid mechanics perspective, this works primarily by:

htw-L1-Q10 multiple-choice

Municipal water pressure is typically 40-60 psi at the tap. If you partially close the faucet valve, the flow rate decreases because:

htw-L1-Q11 fill-blank

A single-handle kitchen faucet uses a _____ valve that controls both flow rate and hot/cold mixing ratio with one lever movement.

htw-L1-Q12 multiple-choice

A sharp kitchen knife cuts more easily than a dull one because:

htw-L1-Q13 true-false

Ceramic kitchen knives are harder than steel knives but more brittle — they hold an edge longer but can shatter if dropped on a hard floor.

htw-L1-Q14 multiple-choice

A knife blade is essentially a wedge. When you push a knife down into food, the wedge converts vertical force into:

htw-L1-Q15 multiple-choice

A microwave oven heats food primarily through:

htw-L1-Q16 true-false

Microwave ovens can produce hot spots and cold spots in food because the standing electromagnetic waves inside the cavity create nodes (zero amplitude) and antinodes (maximum amplitude).

htw-L1-Q17 fill-blank

In a conventional oven, heat reaches the food's interior from its hot surface via _____, which is slow because food has low thermal conductivity. Microwaves bypass this entirely by heating volumetrically.

htw-L1-Q18 multiple-choice

Copper-bottomed pots heat food more evenly than plain stainless steel pots because:

htw-L1-Q19 multiple-choice

When boiling water in a pot, the primary heat transfer mechanism within the water itself is:

htw-L1-Q20 multiple-choice

Fourier's law (q = -k dT/dx) governs heat transfer through the solid base of a pot on a stove. If you double the pot base thickness while keeping everything else the same, the heat flow rate through the base will:

The Car You Drive 20

htw-L2-Q1 multiple-choice

The four strokes of a gasoline engine follow the Otto cycle. Which stroke performs useful work?

htw-L2-Q2 multiple-choice

The thermal efficiency of an ideal Otto cycle depends primarily on:

htw-L2-Q3 true-false

Diesel engines are more thermally efficient than gasoline engines primarily because they operate at higher compression ratios.

htw-L2-Q4 fill-blank

In the Otto cycle, both heat addition and heat rejection occur at constant _____, which distinguishes it from the Diesel cycle where heat is added at constant pressure.

htw-L2-Q5 multiple-choice

A car suspension consists of a spring and a shock absorber (damper) in parallel. The damper's primary role is to:

htw-L2-Q6 multiple-choice

A car suspension is designed to be underdamped. What does this mean in vibration terms?

htw-L2-Q7 fill-blank

The natural frequency of a car's suspension is approximately f = (1/2π)√(k/m), where k is the _____ and m is the sprung mass.

htw-L2-Q8 multiple-choice

When you press the brake pedal and the car decelerates from 100 km/h to 0, the kinetic energy of the car is:

htw-L2-Q9 true-false

Disc brakes are preferred over drum brakes for front axles because disc brakes dissipate heat more effectively — the rotor is exposed to airflow on both sides.

htw-L2-Q10 multiple-choice

Braking force between the tire and road depends on the friction equation F = μN. During hard braking with weight transfer to the front, the front brakes can generate more stopping force because:

htw-L2-Q11 multiple-choice

In first gear, a transmission provides high torque but low speed at the wheels. This is because:

htw-L2-Q12 fill-blank

A gear ratio of 3:1 means the output shaft has _____ times the torque but one-third the rotational speed of the input shaft.

htw-L2-Q13 true-false

An automatic transmission's torque converter can multiply engine torque at low speeds, acting like a fluid coupling with a stator that redirects flow.

htw-L2-Q14 multiple-choice

The contact patch (tire footprint) is the area where the tire touches the road. For a tire supporting 4000 N of load inflated to 200 kPa, the approximate contact patch area is:

htw-L2-Q15 multiple-choice

Hydroplaning occurs when a car drives through standing water and the tires lose contact with the road. From a fluid mechanics perspective, this happens because:

htw-L2-Q16 fill-blank

Tire inflation pressure creates _____ stress in the tire sidewall, similar to a thin-walled pressure vessel, which is why underinflated tires flex excessively and overheat.

htw-L2-Q17 multiple-choice

Power steering reduces the effort needed to turn the wheel. In a hydraulic power steering system, the assist comes from:

htw-L2-Q18 multiple-choice

Ackermann steering geometry ensures that during a turn:

htw-L2-Q19 multiple-choice

An exhaust manifold experiences significant thermal expansion because exhaust gases can reach 700-900°C. Engineers accommodate this expansion by:

htw-L2-Q20 true-false

Excessive exhaust backpressure reduces engine performance because it increases the pumping work the engine must do during the exhaust stroke, leaving less net work available at the crankshaft.

Around the House 20

htw-L3-Q1 multiple-choice

A door hinge allows the door to rotate about a fixed axis. In structural analysis, what type of support does a hinge pin represent?

htw-L3-Q2 multiple-choice

A hydraulic door closer exerts a closing torque of 8 N·m. If the door handle is 0.9 m from the hinge, what minimum force must you apply at the handle to hold the door open?

htw-L3-Q3 multiple-choice

A toilet flushes using a siphon. What primarily drives the water over the siphon crest and into the drain?

htw-L3-Q4 true-false

A toilet fill valve uses a float mechanism that acts as a feedback control system: the rising water level lifts the float, which closes the valve to stop filling — analogous to a proportional controller.

htw-L3-Q5 multiple-choice

In a split air conditioning system, the indoor unit contains the evaporator and the outdoor unit contains the condenser. Why does the indoor unit cool the room?

htw-L3-Q6 fill-blank

The Coefficient of Performance (COP) of a heat pump for cooling equals the _____ removed from the cold space divided by the _____ input to the compressor.

htw-L3-Q7 multiple-choice

A home thermostat turns the AC compressor on when temperature exceeds the setpoint and off when it drops below. What type of control action is this?

htw-L3-Q8 true-false

A standard household light switch uses a snap-action (over-center) mechanism with a spring to ensure rapid contact transition, which minimizes electrical arcing.

htw-L3-Q9 multiple-choice

In a snap-action light switch, the mechanism has two stable positions (on and off) with an unstable equilibrium between them. What is this type of mechanism called?

htw-L3-Q10 multiple-choice

During the spin cycle, a washing machine removes water from clothes by spinning the drum at high speed. What force drives the water out through the drum perforations?

htw-L3-Q11 fill-blank

Washing machines have rubber feet or spring mounts to reduce _____ transmitted to the floor, a technique known as vibration _____.

htw-L3-Q12 true-false

An unbalanced load in a spinning washing machine drum can cause dangerous resonance if the spin-up speed passes through the drum's natural frequency.

htw-L3-Q13 multiple-choice

A living room floor is designed to carry furniture and occupants. In structural engineering, how is this type of loading best classified?

htw-L3-Q14 multiple-choice

Building codes limit floor deflection to L/360 (span/360) under live load. Why is this deflection limit important even though the floor is still structurally safe?

htw-L3-Q15 fill-blank

Double-glazed windows reduce heat loss by trapping a layer of _____ between two panes, which has low thermal _____ and thus resists heat flow.

htw-L3-Q16 multiple-choice

Window glass expands when heated by sunlight. If the glass is rigidly constrained in the frame with no expansion gap, what type of stress develops?

htw-L3-Q17 true-false

The U-value of a window measures its insulating ability: a lower U-value means better insulation and less heat loss.

htw-L3-Q18 fill-blank

When you push a door at its handle (far from the hinge), it opens easily. Pushing near the hinge requires much more force because the _____ arm is shorter, producing less _____ for the same force.

htw-L3-Q19 multiple-choice

An air conditioner with COP = 4 is cooling a room. For every 1 kW of electrical power consumed by the compressor, how much total heat is rejected by the outdoor condenser?

htw-L3-Q20 multiple-choice

A siphon cannot lift water higher than approximately 10.3 m at sea level. What fundamental principle explains this height limit?

Tools & Workshop 20

htw-L4-Q1 multiple-choice

A bolt requires 60 N·m of torque to loosen. You have a wrench that is 0.3 m long but cannot generate enough force. Adding a "cheater pipe" extends the handle to 0.6 m. By what factor does the required hand force decrease?

htw-L4-Q2 true-false

When tightening a bolt with a torque wrench, the applied torque directly equals the clamping force times the bolt diameter — friction plays a negligible role.

htw-L4-Q3 multiple-choice

A drill bit cutting through metal experiences a resisting torque. If you reduce the drill's rotational speed (RPM) while keeping the motor power constant, what happens to the available torque at the bit?

htw-L4-Q4 fill-blank

A drill chuck grips the bit using three jaws that converge via a _____ thread mechanism, converting rotational motion into _____ clamping force.

htw-L4-Q5 multiple-choice

A hydraulic jack has a small piston of area 5 cm² and a large piston of area 50 cm². If you apply 100 N of force to the small piston, what force does the large piston exert?

htw-L4-Q6 multiple-choice

Hydraulic systems use oil rather than air as the working fluid. What property of oil is most critical for a hydraulic jack to function properly?

htw-L4-Q7 true-false

A hydraulic jack with a 20:1 area ratio can lift 20 times the input force, but the small piston must be pumped through 20 times the distance that the large piston rises.

htw-L4-Q8 multiple-choice

A C-clamp uses a screw thread to generate clamping force. If the screw has a pitch of 2 mm and you apply a force of 20 N at a handle radius of 80 mm, what approximate clamping force is generated (ignoring friction)?

htw-L4-Q9 fill-blank

A screw thread is self-locking when the _____ angle exceeds the lead angle, meaning the clamped part cannot push the screw back open on its own.

htw-L4-Q10 multiple-choice

Standard pliers have the fulcrum (pivot) between the effort (handles) and the load (jaws). What class of lever is this?

htw-L4-Q11 multiple-choice

A pair of pliers has handles 20 cm long and jaws 5 cm long (both measured from the pivot). If you squeeze the handles with 40 N, what approximate gripping force do the jaws exert?

htw-L4-Q12 true-false

Tweezers are a third-class lever because the effort (your finger force) is applied between the fulcrum (the joined end) and the load (the tip), meaning they always have a mechanical advantage less than 1.

htw-L4-Q13 multiple-choice

A tape measure retracts automatically because of a coiled spring inside. As you pull the tape out further, the retraction force:

htw-L4-Q14 fill-blank

The coiled spring in a tape measure stores _____ energy when the tape is extended, which is converted to _____ energy of the retracting tape when released.

htw-L4-Q15 multiple-choice

A screwdriver with a larger-diameter handle is easier to turn than one with a thin handle, even when driving the same screw. Why?

htw-L4-Q16 multiple-choice

A wood screw with a finer thread pitch (smaller distance between threads) compared to a coarse-pitch screw of the same diameter will:

htw-L4-Q17 true-false

A screw with a very steep lead angle (like an ACME power screw) can back-drive under load, meaning the load can push the screw out without any applied torque. This makes it unsuitable for applications like a car jack without a braking mechanism.

htw-L4-Q18 multiple-choice

A spirit level (bubble level) works because the bubble inside the curved vial always moves to the highest point. What principle governs the bubble's position?

htw-L4-Q19 fill-blank

The vial of a spirit _____ is slightly curved (barrel-shaped) so that the bubble acts as an _____, seeking the highest point of the vial when the surface is.

htw-L4-Q20 multiple-choice

You are using a spirit level inside an elevator that is accelerating upward. How does the accelerating reference frame affect the level's reading?

Sports & Outdoors 20

htw-L5-Q1 multiple-choice

A bicycle has a 48-tooth chainring and a 16-tooth rear cog. What is the gear ratio, and what does it mean physically?

htw-L5-Q2 multiple-choice

Why are bicycle spokes installed with pretension (tightened before any load is applied)?

htw-L5-Q3 true-false

On a bicycle, shifting to a smaller rear cog (fewer teeth) makes pedaling easier because the gear ratio decreases.

htw-L5-Q4 fill-blank

A bicycle chain transmits force through _____. The chain links on the tight side (pulling side) carry _____ tension than the slack side.

htw-L5-Q5 multiple-choice

The "sweet spot" of a tennis racket is the point where hitting feels effortless with minimal vibration in the handle. What engineering principle explains this?

htw-L5-Q6 multiple-choice

Modern tennis rackets use carbon fiber composites instead of wood. What is the primary engineering advantage?

htw-L5-Q7 multiple-choice

A roller coaster car reaches the top of a vertical loop. What is the minimum condition for the car to maintain contact with the track at the top?

htw-L5-Q8 true-false

On a frictionless roller coaster, a car that starts from rest at height h will always reach the same maximum speed at the bottom, regardless of the track shape.

htw-L5-Q9 multiple-choice

When a skateboarder rides through a curved half-pipe, they feel "heavier" at the bottom of the curve. Why?

htw-L5-Q10 multiple-choice

A diving board is a classic example of what type of structural element, and where does maximum bending stress occur?

htw-L5-Q11 fill-blank

When a diver stands at the end of a diving board and bounces, the board oscillates at its _____. The energy stored in the bent board is _____ energy.

htw-L5-Q12 multiple-choice

Bicycle and football helmets use crushable foam liners to protect the head during impact. What is the engineering principle behind this?

htw-L5-Q13 true-false

A helmet foam liner should be highly elastic (like a rubber ball) so it can be reused after each impact.

htw-L5-Q14 multiple-choice

Competitive swimmers shave their bodies and wear smooth suits to reduce drag. Which type of drag are they primarily targeting?

htw-L5-Q15 fill-blank

The Reynolds number for a swimmer is approximately 10⁶, indicating the flow is _____. Streamlining the body profile primarily reduces _____ drag.

htw-L5-Q16 multiple-choice

Golf club heads have evolved from small steel blocks to large hollow titanium drivers. What engineering property does a larger head improve?

htw-L5-Q17 multiple-choice

The coefficient of restitution (COR) for a golf ball striking a club face is regulated to a maximum of 0.83. What does COR physically represent?

htw-L5-Q18 true-false

In a golf swing, doubling the club head speed at impact will exactly double the ball's launch speed.

htw-L5-Q19 fill-blank

A roller coaster loop is often shaped as a _____ (not a perfect circle) so that the centripetal acceleration at the top is manageable without requiring excessive _____.

htw-L5-Q20 multiple-choice

A rowing team wants to maximize boat speed. The drag force on the hull is proportional to v². If they double their combined power output, by what factor does the steady-state speed increase?

Buildings & Infrastructure 20

htw-L6-Q1 multiple-choice

Most elevators use a counterweight that equals the cab weight plus about 40% of the maximum passenger load. Why not 50%?

htw-L6-Q2 multiple-choice

An elevator cable supports a 2000 kg cab with a 1500 kg counterweight. When the cab accelerates upward at 1.5 m/s², what is the net force the motor must provide?

htw-L6-Q3 true-false

Elevator safety brakes work by gripping the guide rails when the cable tension drops to zero (cable breaks), using a centrifugal governor that detects overspeed.

htw-L6-Q4 fill-blank

An elevator uses a _____ system where the counterweight moves _____ when the cab moves up, keeping the cable tension manageable.

htw-L6-Q5 multiple-choice

In a simple truss bridge, how do the top chord and bottom chord members carry loads?

htw-L6-Q6 multiple-choice

Why can an arch bridge span farther than a simple beam bridge of the same material and cross-section?

htw-L6-Q7 true-false

In a suspension bridge, the main cables are in tension and the towers are in compression.

htw-L6-Q8 multiple-choice

A water tower provides water pressure to a town. If the water surface is 40 m above a house's faucet, what is the approximate water pressure at the faucet (ρ_water = 1000 kg/m³)?

htw-L6-Q9 fill-blank

A water tower works by converting gravitational _____ energy of elevated water into _____ energy at the point of use.

htw-L6-Q10 multiple-choice

An HVAC engineer must choose between a 30 cm and a 20 cm diameter round duct for the same airflow. How does the pressure drop compare?

htw-L6-Q11 multiple-choice

In HVAC duct design, the Reynolds number is used to determine whether the airflow is laminar or turbulent. For typical duct conditions (Re > 4000), what does this mean for the friction factor?

htw-L6-Q12 multiple-choice

A mobile crane must not tip over when lifting a load. What is the stability criterion based on moments about the tipping edge?

htw-L6-Q13 true-false

A crane's maximum lifting capacity remains constant regardless of the boom angle and reach distance.

htw-L6-Q14 fill-blank

A crane boom acts as a _____ beam under combined axial compression and bending. The critical failure mode for a long slender boom is _____.

htw-L6-Q15 multiple-choice

Hydrostatic pressure on a dam increases linearly with depth. What is the shape of the pressure distribution, and where does the resultant force act?

htw-L6-Q16 multiple-choice

Why are gravity dams built with a much thicker base than top?

htw-L6-Q17 true-false

The total hydrostatic force on a dam doubles when the water depth doubles.

htw-L6-Q18 multiple-choice

An escalator carries 30 passengers (average 75 kg each) up a vertical rise of 10 m. The belt moves at 0.5 m/s along the incline at 30°. What minimum motor power is needed, ignoring friction and efficiency losses?

htw-L6-Q19 fill-blank

For a belt conveyor, the motor must provide enough torque to overcome _____ between the belt and pulleys. The power equation P = _____ × v relates driving force to belt speed.

htw-L6-Q20 multiple-choice

An elevator motor is rated at 15 kW with 85% efficiency. What is the maximum useful power delivered to move the cab?

Everyday Failures 20

htw-L7-Q1 multiple-choice

A copper water pipe bursts during a winter freeze. What is the primary mechanical cause of the failure?

htw-L7-Q2 multiple-choice

PEX tubing is far more resistant to freeze-burst damage than copper pipe. Which material property best explains this advantage?

htw-L7-Q3 multiple-choice

A bolted joint on a vibrating machine keeps loosening despite being initially torqued to spec. What is the dominant loosening mechanism?

htw-L7-Q4 multiple-choice

An engineer applies Loctite threadlocker to a critical bolted joint. How does the adhesive primarily prevent loosening?

htw-L7-Q5 multiple-choice

A bicycle chain link fails after thousands of kilometers with no single overload event. The fracture surface shows beach marks. What failure mode does this indicate?

htw-L7-Q6 multiple-choice

On an S-N (Wöhler) curve for a steel bicycle chain link, what does the "endurance limit" represent?

htw-L7-Q7 multiple-choice

Under-inflated tires are a leading cause of highway blowouts. What failure mechanism does under-inflation promote?

htw-L7-Q8 multiple-choice

A tire rated at 35 psi (gauge) at 20 °C is driven on hot asphalt until the air inside reaches 60 °C. Assuming constant volume, the new gauge pressure is approximately:

htw-L7-Q9 multiple-choice

A steel bolt is used to fasten an aluminum bracket on a boat. Within months the aluminum shows severe pitting while the steel bolt is unaffected. What type of corrosion is occurring?

htw-L7-Q10 multiple-choice

Galvanized steel is coated with zinc. If the coating is scratched and the underlying steel is exposed, what happens?

htw-L7-Q11 multiple-choice

Pouring boiling water into a room-temperature glass cup can crack it, but a Pyrex measuring cup survives the same treatment. What material property gives Pyrex its advantage?

htw-L7-Q12 multiple-choice

A phone screen often cracks from a corner or edge impact rather than a center hit. Which fracture mechanics concept explains this?

htw-L7-Q13 true-false

When you bend a metal paper clip back and forth until it breaks, it fails by low-cycle fatigue — it always breaks where you bend it because that is where the bending stress and plastic strain are greatest.

htw-L7-Q14 true-false

A car radiator hose that has hardened and cracked after years of service has failed primarily due to creep — time-dependent deformation under sustained load at elevated temperature.

htw-L7-Q15 true-false

Pitting corrosion is generally more dangerous than uniform corrosion because pits act as stress concentrators and can lead to unexpected through-wall penetration while overall material loss appears minor.

htw-L7-Q16 true-false

Pressure-treated lumber resists rot because the treatment process makes the wood waterproof, preventing moisture from entering the wood fibers.

htw-L7-Q17 fill-blank

When water freezes inside a sealed pipe, it expands by about _____ %, creating internal pressure. The resulting _____ stress in the pipe wall equals pr/t for a thin-walled cylinder.

htw-L7-Q18 fill-blank

The thermal stress in a fully constrained solid is given by σ = E·_____·ΔT, where E is elastic modulus and ΔT is temperature change. Pyrex resists thermal shock because its coefficient of thermal _____ is very low.

htw-L7-Q19 fill-blank

Bolts loosen most readily under _____ vibration (perpendicular to the bolt axis), which causes relative _____ between mating thread surfaces — an effect demonstrated by Junker.

htw-L7-Q20 fill-blank

According to the Griffith criterion, a crack in a brittle material will propagate when the _____ energy released by crack growth exceeds the energy needed to create new _____.

🏭

Materials & Manufacturing

180 diagrams across 18 lessons

Tensile Test Basics 9

u7-L1-Q1 multiple-choice

Why is reduction in area often a better ductility measure than percent elongation?

u7-L1-Q2 multiple-choice

Which hardness test uses a diamond pyramid for thin coatings and micro-phases?

u7-L1-Q3 true-false

The DBTT is a concern for BCC metals (carbon steel) but not FCC metals (stainless, aluminum).

u7-L1-Q4 multiple-choice

A steam header in a power plant operates at 550°C under constant internal pressure. After 15 years of service, inspection reveals the tube has permanently bulged outward. There are no signs of corrosion or cyclic loading. What failure mechanism is most likely responsible?

u7-L1-Q5 multiple-choice

S-N curve shows endurance limit of 300 MPa. Practical meaning?

u7-L1-Q6 fill-blank

A shaft broke unexpectedly in service. The fracture surface shows smooth, curved "beach marks" radiating from an origin point, with a small rough area of final fracture. This pattern is characteristic of _____ failure.

u7-L1-Q7 multiple-choice

A yield strength of 350 MPa and an ultimate tensile strength of 520 MPa. What is the strain hardening ratio?

u7-L1-Q8 multiple-choice

Round bar specimen, the engineering stress appears to decrease after reaching the UTS. Is the material actually getting weaker?

u7-L1-Q9 multiple-choice

Rank these materials by elastic modulus (highest to lowest):

Hardness, Creep, and Fatigue 10

u7-L1-Q10 true-false

The Rockwell C hardness test (HRC) uses a 1/16-inch steel ball indenter and is commonly used for soft metals like aluminum and brass.

u7-L1-Q11 multiple-choice

A component made of 316 stainless steel will operate at 700°C under moderate sustained load for 20 years. Which material property should govern the design stress?

u7-L1-Q12 multiple-choice

What is the primary difference between creep and stress relaxation?

u7-L1-Q13 fill-blank

The Brinell hardness test uses a hardened steel or tungsten carbide ball pressed into the surface under heavy load. The hardness number is calculated from the load divided by the curved surface area of the _____.

u7-L1-Q14 multiple-choice

A high-cycle fatigue failure initiated at a machining mark on the surface of a hardened shaft. What surface treatment could have most effectively prevented this failure?

u7-L1-Q15 multiple-choice

An engineer compares two steels for a bridge. Steel A: YS = 250 MPa, Charpy = 100 J at -40°C. Steel B: YS = 500 MPa, Charpy = 20 J at -40°C. Which is more appropriate and why?

u7-L1-Q16 true-false

Aluminum alloys have a true endurance limit like carbon steels.

u7-L1-Q17 multiple-choice

What does the area under the stress-strain curve up to fracture represent?

u7-L1-Q18 multiple-choice

A creep test at 600°C shows three distinct stages. In which stage does the creep rate reach a minimum and remain approximately constant?

u7-L1-Q19 multiple-choice

What is the key difference between a Charpy and an Izod impact test?

Fracture and Toughness 11

u7-L1-Q20 true-false

Modulus of resilience = area under the curve up to fracture.

u7-L1-Q21 multiple-choice

Pressure vessel at -196°C (liquid nitrogen). Best material choice?

u7-L1-Q22 multiple-choice

A Brinell hardness test on gray cast iron yields HB = 200. Using the relationship UTS ≈ 3.45 × HB (MPa), can you reliably estimate the UTS of the cast iron?

u7-L1-Q23 fill-blank

The _____ offset method is used to determine the _____ strength of materials that do not exhibit a clear yield point. A line is drawn parallel to the elastic region, offset by a specific strain value.

u7-L1-Q24 multiple-choice

What factor most reduces a component's endurance limit vs a polished lab specimen?

u7-L1-Q25 true-false

Poisson's ratio of a perfectly incompressible material (like rubber under small strains) is 0.5, meaning it conserves volume exactly during elastic deformation.

u7-L1-Q26 multiple-choice

A leaf spring made of 5160 steel snapped in service. Fractographic examination shows ratchet marks at multiple initiation sites on the tension surface. What do multiple initiation sites indicate?

u7-L1-Q27 multiple-choice

How does a stress-rupture test differ from a standard creep test?

u7-L1-Q28 multiple-choice

A hardness traverse across a case-carburized gear tooth shows 60 HRC at the surface dropping to 30 HRC at the core. Why is this gradient desirable?

u7-L1-Q29 true-false

K_IC generally increases with increasing yield strength.

u7-L1-Q30 fill-blank

The critical value of stress intensity factor at which a crack propagates unstably under plane strain conditions is called K_____ (the plane-strain fracture toughness).

Iron-Carbon Diagram 9

u7-L2-Q1 multiple-choice

Why can austenite (FCC iron) dissolve much more carbon than ferrite (BCC iron)?

u7-L2-Q2 multiple-choice

1045 steel quenched then tempered at 400°C. Purpose of tempering?

u7-L2-Q3 true-false

The eutectoid composition of the Fe-C system is 0.8 wt% carbon at 727°C

u7-L2-Q4 multiple-choice

A machinist quenched a 1045 steel part in water and achieved full hardness, but when the same procedure was used on a larger part of the same steel, the core remained soft. What is the metallurgical explanation?

u7-L2-Q5 multiple-choice

A welded structural frame made of 1020 steel has uneven grain structure and residual stresses from welding. The engineer wants to refine the microstructure and relieve stresses, but full annealing takes too long. What heat treatment would you recommend and why?

u7-L2-Q6 fill-blank

When tempering a quenched steel at progressively higher temperatures, toughness increases but hardness decreases. However, there is a range around 250-350°C that should be avoided for certain alloy steels because it causes a phenomenon called temper _____.

u7-L2-Q7 multiple-choice

What microstructure results when a eutectoid steel is cooled from the austenite region at a rate fast enough to bypass the pearlite nose on the TTT diagram but held at 300-350°C?

u7-L2-Q8 multiple-choice

Maximum carbon solubility in austenite at the eutectic temperature?

u7-L2-Q9 true-false

Martensite is an equilibrium phase that appears on the Fe-C phase diagram.

Heat Treatment Processes 11

u7-L2-Q10 multiple-choice

1080 eutectoid steel slowly cooled from 800°C. Microstructure and hardness?

u7-L2-Q11 multiple-choice

What is the purpose of adding molybdenum to alloy steels like 4140 and 4340?

u7-L2-Q12 multiple-choice

A Jominy end-quench test bar shows 60 HRC at the quenched end and drops to 25 HRC at 25 mm from the quenched end. What does this indicate about the steel?

u7-L2-Q13 fill-blank

Fe-C diagram: alloys above _____ wt% C are cast _____, not steels.

u7-L2-Q14 multiple-choice

Quenched a 1045 steel part in water without tempering. The part cracked on the shelf overnight. What is the most likely cause of this delayed cracking?

u7-L2-Q15 multiple-choice

Process annealing vs full annealing of steel?

u7-L2-Q16 true-false

Spheroidizing is a heat treatment that produces the softest and most ductile form of a given steel, making it easier to machine or cold-form.

u7-L2-Q17 multiple-choice

How does a CCT diagram differ from a TTT diagram?

u7-L2-Q18 multiple-choice

An engineer needs to case-harden a low-carbon steel (1020) gear to achieve a hard, wear-resistant surface while maintaining a tough core. Which process is most appropriate?

u7-L2-Q19 multiple-choice

What is retained austenite and why is it a concern?

u7-L2-Q20 true-false

Nitriding requires quenching to achieve surface hardness.

Advanced Heat Treatment 10

u7-L2-Q21 multiple-choice

A hypoeutectoid steel (0.4% C) is heated to 780°C and slowly cooled. What microstructural constituents will be present at room temperature and in what order did they form?

u7-L2-Q22 multiple-choice

Why add Cr and Ni to steel for heat treatment?

u7-L2-Q23 fill-blank

Austempering is an isothermal heat treatment where steel is quenched to a temperature above Ms (typically 250-400°C) and held to form _____, which provides an excellent combination of strength and toughness.

u7-L2-Q24 multiple-choice

What is sensitization in austenitic stainless and how to prevent it?

u7-L2-Q25 true-false

Induction hardening can be used to selectively harden specific surface areas of a medium-carbon steel part without affecting the entire component.

u7-L2-Q26 multiple-choice

Upper bainite vs lower bainite: microstructure and properties?

u7-L2-Q27 multiple-choice

A part made from D2 tool steel (high-carbon, high-chromium) was quenched and tempered but shows lower hardness than expected. Cryogenic treatment at -196°C is proposed. What is the rationale?

u7-L2-Q28 multiple-choice

Role of Ms temperature in heat treatment? Effect of carbon content?

u7-L2-Q29 true-false

Normalizing and full annealing both involve heating above the upper critical temperature, but normalizing uses air cooling while full annealing uses slow furnace cooling.

u7-L2-Q30 fill-blank

The iron-carbon phase diagram shows that the _____ reaction occurs at 4.3 wt% C and 1147°C, where liquid transforms simultaneously into austenite and cementite. This mixture is called _____.

Casting Processes 9

u7-L3-Q1 multiple-choice

A structural bracket must withstand a static tensile load of 45 kN, operate at temperatures up to 150°C, weigh as little as possible, and be produced in batches of 5,000 units/year. The bracket has a complex 3D geometry with internal ribs and thin walls (2.5 mm). Considering material properties, manufacturability, and cost, which material + process combination is the best choice?

u7-L3-Q2 multiple-choice

A welded T-joint on a thick rolled steel plate failed by cracking parallel to the plate surface in the HAZ, beneath the weld. The crack surfaces show a layered, woody appearance. What is this failure mode?

In casting, should the riser solidify before or after the casting?

Cast housing and need to decide between sand casting and investment casting. The part has thin walls (3 mm), internal passages, tight tolerances (±0.2 mm), and annual volume of 500 units.

u7-L3-Q8 multiple-choice

Hot tears (hot cracking) in castings occur during solidification when thermal contraction stresses exceed the strength of the partially solidified metal.

Deep drawing is a sheet metal forming process where a flat blank is drawn into a cylindrical cup shape without any change in the blank thickness.

Turning, Milling, and Drilling 9

u7-L4-Q1 multiple-choice

Facing a large-diameter workpiece from the outside edge toward the center. If the spindle speed is held constant (G97), what happens to the cutting conditions as the tool moves inward?

u7-L4-Q2 multiple-choice

Which type of milling generally produces a better surface finish and is preferred when machine rigidity allows?

u7-L4-Q3 true-false

BUE during machining protects the tool and improves surface finish.

u7-L4-Q4 multiple-choice

That tool life has dropped dramatically on a job that was running fine last month. The material, tooling, and program are unchanged. Walk through your troubleshooting process. What is the most likely root cause?

u7-L4-Q5 multiple-choice

u7-L4-Q7 multiple-choice

u7-L4-Q8 multiple-choice

What is the primary purpose of cutting fluid in machining operations?

u7-L4-Q9 true-false

G00 is the rapid positioning command (non-cutting move) and G01 is the linear interpolation command (cutting move at a specified feed rate).

Tool Wear and Surface Finish 11

u7-L4-Q10 multiple-choice

G02 = _____ circular arc, G03 = _____ circular arc.

Ceramic tools are ideal for interrupted cuts due to high toughness.

Electrical discharge machining (EDM) can only machine electrically conductive materials because it relies on spark erosion between the tool electrode and the workpiece.

u7-L4-Q28 multiple-choice

u7-L4-Q29 true-false

In milling, the chip load (feed per tooth) can be calculated as fz = Vf / (N × z), where Vf is the table feed rate, N is the spindle speed, and z is the number of teeth on the cutter.

The G96 command activates constant _____ speed mode, which automatically adjusts RPM as the diameter changes during facing operations.

Additive Manufacturing 9

u7-L5-Q1 multiple-choice

Your team is considering metal 3D printing (DMLS/SLM) for a titanium aerospace bracket currently machined from billet. The machined version wastes 90% of the raw material. What factors would you evaluate to decide if switching to additive manufacturing is justified?

u7-L5-Q2 multiple-choice

In injection molding, what is the primary reason for including draft angles on the vertical walls of a part?

u7-L5-Q3 true-false

The minimum recommended inside bend radius for most ductile metals is equal to the material thickness (1T bend radius).

u7-L5-Q4 multiple-choice

A designer hands you a part drawing with uniform 3 mm wall thickness, sharp internal corners, and a deep narrow slot (1 mm wide × 15 mm deep). They want it injection molded in ABS. What DFM feedback would you give, and why?

u7-L5-Q5 multiple-choice

The designer specified a tight tolerance (±0.01 mm) on a non-critical internal bore that interfaces with nothing. The machinist says this requires internal grinding, adding $15/part. How would you approach this conversation with the designer?

u7-L5-Q6 fill-blank

Uneven wall thickness causes one side to cool and shrink faster than the other, pulling the part surface inward and creating a cosmetic defect called a _____ mark.

u7-L5-Q7 multiple-choice

What is the key difference between SLA (Stereolithography) and SLS (Selective Laser Sintering) in terms of material and support structures?

u7-L5-Q8 multiple-choice

Why are parts typically weaker in the Z-direction (build direction) compared to the X-Y plane?

u7-L5-Q9 true-false

Increasing the wall thickness uniformly throughout a part generally reduces cycle time because the thicker walls cool more uniformly.

Injection Molding and Sheet Metal 10

u7-L5-Q15 multiple-choice

u7-L5-Q16 true-false

Topology optimization uses software algorithms to determine the optimal material distribution within a design space, often producing organic-looking shapes that are ideally suited for additive manufacturing.

u7-L5-Q17 multiple-choice

In injection molding, what are weld lines (knit lines) and when do they form?

u7-L5-Q18 multiple-choice

u7-L5-Q19 multiple-choice

A boss (cylindrical protrusion for a screw) on an injection-molded part. What are the DFM guidelines for boss design?

Design for Manufacturing 11

u7-L5-Q20 true-false

The minimum distance between a hole edge and a bend line should be at least 2-3 times the material thickness plus the bend radius to prevent the hole from distorting during bending.

u7-L5-Q21 multiple-choice

What are the main differences between hot runner and cold runner systems in injection molding?

u7-L5-Q22 multiple-choice

In metal 3D printing (SLM/DMLS), why is post-build heat treatment (stress relief) almost always required?

u7-L5-Q23 fill-blank

The cosmetic defect that appears as thin, dark, meandering lines on the part surface, caused by trapped gases _____ at the end of fill, is called a _____ mark.

u7-L5-Q24 multiple-choice

What is the primary advantage of multi-jet fusion (MJF) over SLS for polymer part production?

u7-L5-Q25 true-false

Bend relief cuts are required at the end of a bend line that terminates at an edge or another feature, to prevent tearing of the material.

u7-L5-Q26 multiple-choice

An injection molder observes that parts are sticking in the mold cavity side instead of pulling out with the core side during mold opening. What is the most likely cause?

u7-L5-Q27 multiple-choice

What is the significance of the glass transition temperature (Tg) in selecting polymers for injection molding applications?

u7-L5-Q28 multiple-choice

A company currently CNC machines aluminum brackets in batches of 50. They are considering switching to FDM printing with carbon-fiber-filled nylon. What are the key technical trade-offs?

u7-L5-Q29 true-false

The clamping force must exceed the force generated by the injection pressure acting on the projected area of the part, otherwise the mold will open and cause flash.

u7-L5-Q30 fill-blank

The process of cutting a flat pattern from a sheet using a punch and die set is called _____ (also known as stamping or die cutting).

Welding Processes 9

u7-L6-Q1 multiple-choice

A fabrication shop must weld 6 mm thick 304 stainless steel panels for a food-processing vessel requiring high-quality, porosity-free welds with minimal spatter and excellent corrosion resistance in the weld zone. Which welding process is the best choice?

u7-L6-Q2 multiple-choice

About to join two plates of AISI 4140 steel (0.40% C, Cr-Mo alloy). You calculate the carbon equivalent (CE) at 0.72. What precautions must be taken before, during, and after welding, and why?

u7-L6-Q3 multiple-choice

A welded pressure vessel fails a radiographic (RT) inspection. The film shows dark, rounded indications scattered within the weld. What is the most likely defect?

u7-L6-Q4 multiple-choice

A fillet weld connecting a bracket to a column must resist a static shear load of 90 kN. The weld runs along both sides of the bracket for 150 mm each side (total weld length = 300 mm). Using an E70 electrode (allowable shear stress on throat = 0.3 × 482 = 145 MPa per AWS D1.1), what is the minimum required fillet weld leg size?

u7-L6-Q5 true-false

Welded aluminum frame for a marine application. Using 6061-T6 is a good choice because, unlike steel, aluminum does not lose strength in the weld heat-affected zone.

u7-L6-Q6 fill-blank

Aluminum bracket to a steel frame. Fusion welding is not feasible because of brittle _____ compounds forming at the interface. The most practical joining method for this dissimilar-metal joint is _____ fastening (using bolts or rivets with insulating bushings to prevent galvanic corrosion).

u7-L6-Q7 multiple-choice

What is the primary difference between GMAW (MIG) and FCAW (Flux-Cored Arc Welding)?

u7-L6-Q8 multiple-choice

A weld inspection reveals lack of fusion defects along the sidewall of a V-groove butt joint. What is the most likely cause and correction?

u7-L6-Q9 true-false

Preheating before welding slows the cooling rate in the HAZ, reducing the risk of hydrogen-induced cracking in susceptible steels.

HAZ and Weld Defects 11

u7-L6-Q10 multiple-choice

An engineer needs to weld 3 mm thick 6061-T6 aluminum sheets for a structural frame. Which filler metal should be selected: ER4043 or ER5356?

u7-L6-Q11 multiple-choice

What is the heat-affected zone (HAZ) and why is it often the weakest part of a welded joint?

u7-L6-Q12 multiple-choice

What welding distortion pattern is typically observed in a long butt weld between two flat plates?

u7-L6-Q13 fill-blank

In arc welding, the measure of energy input per unit length of weld is called heat _____, typically expressed in kJ/mm.

u7-L6-Q14 multiple-choice

Excessive spatter during GMAW (MIG) welding of mild steel. What are the most likely causes and corrections?

u7-L6-Q15 multiple-choice

What is the difference between a groove weld and a fillet weld, and when is each used?

u7-L6-Q16 true-false

Submerged arc welding (SAW) is suitable for all welding positions including vertical-up and overhead.

u7-L6-Q17 multiple-choice

What is the purpose of back-gouging the root of a double-V groove weld before welding the second side?

u7-L6-Q18 multiple-choice

Friction stir welding (FSW) joins materials without melting them. What is its primary advantage and main limitation?

u7-L6-Q19 multiple-choice

What NDT method is best suited for detecting surface-breaking cracks in ferromagnetic materials?

u7-L6-Q20 true-false

The minimum overlap should be at least 5 times the thickness of the thinner member to ensure adequate load transfer.

Joint Design and Inspection 10

u7-L6-Q21 multiple-choice

A weld procedure specification (WPS) for a critical pressure vessel weld is being developed. What are the essential variables that must be qualified by procedure qualification testing (PQR)?

u7-L6-Q22 multiple-choice

What is the purpose of post-weld heat treatment (PWHT) and for which applications is it typically required?

u7-L6-Q23 fill-blank

The effective load-carrying dimension of a fillet weld is the _____ of the weld, equal to 0.707 times the _____ size for an equal-leg fillet.

u7-L6-Q24 multiple-choice

Welding a 50 mm thick ASTM A516 Grade 70 pressure vessel plate. The calculated carbon equivalent is 0.48. What minimum preheat temperature would you recommend?

u7-L6-Q25 true-false

Laser beam welding (LBW) produces a very narrow weld with a high depth-to-width ratio and minimal HAZ compared to arc welding processes.

u7-L6-Q26 multiple-choice

What is the difference between undercut and underfill as weld defects?

u7-L6-Q27 multiple-choice

Why is acetylene (C₂H₂) preferred over other fuel gases for oxy-fuel cutting of steel?

u7-L6-Q28 multiple-choice

What are the three essential conditions for hydrogen-induced cracking (cold cracking) in welds?

u7-L6-Q29 true-false

Resistance spot welding (RSW) requires filler metal to be added between the sheets being joined.

u7-L6-Q30 fill-blank

When a fillet weld is loaded in shear parallel to the weld axis (longitudinal loading), the failure plane passes through the weld _____, which is the minimum cross-sectional dimension of the weld.

📏

GD&T & Tolerancing

40 diagrams across 11 lessons

Tolerance Fundamentals 7

u9-L1-Q10 true-false

A transition fit can result in either a small clearance or a small interference between mating parts, depending on the actual?

u9-L1-Q4 multiple-choice

A dimension is specified as 50 +0.05/-0.10 mm. What is the tolerance, and is it bilateral or unilateral?

u9-L1-Q14 multiple-choice

A designer specifies dia 30 +0.021/+0.000 mm for a bore. What type of tolerancing is this?

u9-L1-Q6 fill-blank

The minimum intended difference in size between mating parts in a clearance fit is called the _____.

u9-L1-Q18 fill-blank

A fit where the tolerance zones of the hole and shaft overlap, potentially resulting in either clearance or interference, is _____?

u9-L1-Q3 multiple-choice

A drawing specifies dia 50 +0.000/-0.050 mm for a bore and dia 50 +0.025/+0.050 mm for the mating shaft. What type of fit is this?

u9-L1-Q1 multiple-choice

Why use an interference fit for the inner ring on a rotating shaft, but a clearance fit for the outer ring in the housing?

ISO Fit System 7

u9-L1-Q7 multiple-choice

In the ISO hole-basis system, what does the designation H7/g6 represent?

u9-L1-Q24 fill-blank

In the ISO system, an "H" hole has its _____ deviation equal to zero, while an "h" shaft has its _____ deviation equal to zero.

u9-L1-Q9 multiple-choice

A shaft is specified as dia 25 h6. What are the upper and lower deviations of this shaft?

u9-L1-Q8 multiple-choice

Difference between the hole-basis and shaft-basis fit systems, and when would you prefer the shaft-basis system?

u9-L1-Q12 multiple-choice

What is the relationship between tolerance grade number (IT number) and the magnitude of the tolerance?

u9-L1-Q13 true-false

In the ISO system, for a given IT grade, the tolerance value is constant regardless of the nominal diameter.

u9-L1-Q19 multiple-choice

Why is the H7/H6 hole tolerance more commonly used than custom fundamental deviations (e.g., G7, K7)?

Applied Fits & Standards 7

u9-L1-Q29 multiple-choice

The pin must be easy to push in by hand but have negligible clearance. Which fit is most appropriate?

u9-L1-Q11 multiple-choice

You need to select a fit for a gear hub on a keyed shaft. Which fit type is most appropriate?

u9-L1-Q5 multiple-choice

A drawing has an IT6 tolerance on a rough cast surface that gets no machining. Why is this a problem?

u9-L1-Q26 multiple-choice

A drawing callout shows dia 20 +/-0.05 with a note "INTERPRET PER ASME Y14.5-2018." Under Rule #1, what does this imply?

u9-L1-Q27 true-false

The ISO default for regular features of size is the Envelope Principle (perfect form at MMC), the same as ASME Y14.5 Rule #1.

u9-L1-Q22 multiple-choice

The fit is H7/p6 (light interference) at 20 C. What happens to the fit at higher operating temperature?

u9-L1-Q21 true-false

When specifying tolerances, the designer should always use the tightest tolerance achievable by the manufacturing process.

GD&T Basics 7

u9-L2-Q10 true-false

Form tolerances require datum references in the feature control frame.

u9-L2-Q7 multiple-choice

List the five categories of geometric tolerances defined in ASME Y14.5.

u9-L2-Q6 fill-blank

The condition where a feature of size contains the maximum amount of material is called _____.

u9-L2-Q8 multiple-choice

What is the difference between circularity and cylindricity?

u9-L2-Q2 multiple-choice

What is wrong with applying a flatness callout to a cylindrical bore?

u9-L2-Q1 multiple-choice

Why is position tolerance almost always applied at MMC rather than RFS for clearance-hole bolt patterns?

u9-L2-Q9 multiple-choice

The hole has a size tolerance of dia 10.0-10.5 with position tolerance dia 0.25 at MMC. What is the total positional tolerance when the hole is produced at dia 10.3?

Orientation & Position 4

u9-L2-Q19 multiple-choice

A designer applies a parallelism tolerance of 0.02 to a surface. Must the surface also be flat within 0.02?

u9-L2-Q12 multiple-choice

A perpendicularity tolerance of 0.05 is applied to a flat surface relative to datum A. What is the shape of the tolerance zone?

u9-L2-Q13 multiple-choice

What is the "virtual condition" of an external feature (shaft) specified at dia 20.0 +/- 0.1 with perpendicularity of dia 0.05 at MMC?

u9-L2-Q5 multiple-choice

When should the LMC (Least Material Condition) modifier be used instead of MMC?

Advanced GD&T 2

u9-L2-Q11 multiple-choice

What is the difference between circular runout and total runout?

u9-L2-Q22 multiple-choice

In a composite position tolerance (two rows), what does the lower segment control?

A datum feature and a datum are the same thing.
